question:
DataPower upgrade message Loading firmware failed: Preserve user modified data failed: Insufficient flash space available Could not install firmware image. The system will not reboot. In the process of upgrading my IBM DataPower Gateway I saw the following message:
"Loading firmware failed: Preserve user modified data failed: Insufficient flash space available Could not install firmware image. The system will not reboot."
What does this mean and what are the appropriate next actions?
ground_truth:
Based on the error message you encountered during the DataPower firmware upgrade, it seems that there is insufficient flash space available on the DataPower appliance to complete the firmware upgrade process.

The key points here are:

1. Preserve user modified data failed:
   - This indicates that the DataPower appliance was unable to preserve the existing user-modified configuration and data during the firmware upgrade process.

2. Insufficient flash space available:
   - The appliance does not have enough free flash storage space to accommodate the new firmware image and preserve the existing data.

3. Could not install firmware image:
   - Due to the lack of available flash space, the firmware upgrade process could not be completed successfully.

4. The system will not reboot:
   - As a result of the failed firmware upgrade, the DataPower appliance is unable to reboot and start up with the new firmware.

The appropriate next actions are:

1. Free up space on the DataPower appliance:
   - Identify and delete any unused or unnecessary data, configurations, or log files to make more flash space available.
   - You may need to contact IBM support for guidance on how to free up space on your specific DataPower model.

2. Retry the firmware upgrade:
   - Once you have freed up sufficient flash space, attempt the firmware upgrade process again.
   - Ensure that you have the correct firmware image for your DataPower model and follow the upgrade instructions carefully.

3. If the issue persists, contact IBM support:
   - If you are still unable to successfully upgrade the firmware, reach out to IBM support for further assistance.
   - They may be able to provide additional troubleshooting steps or guidance on resolving the insufficient flash space issue.

It's important to address this issue promptly, as running the DataPower appliance with an outdated firmware can expose it to security vulnerabilities and prevent you from taking advantage of the latest features and bug fixes.

reference_context:
FLASH (ALERT)

ABSTRACT
 Possible abrupt data access loss that can be prevented 

CONTENT
This problem was recently recognized, and can cause the interface adapters to suddenly halt, and close the links. No data internal to the box is lost. 

Recovery option are in recommended order: 

 1. Upgrade system firmware 
 2. Reboot system 
 3. Reset Interface Adapters.


To Prevent Access Loss, please read further. 

On Flashsystem 720/820: 

Code levels less than 6.3.2_p2 are affected. 

 

All Adapter types are affected. FC, IB 

 

Run System Report from Gui Panel, or CLI (system report) 

Open file, check header page information:
===============================================================================
=== System Report ===
=== Fri Mar 06 07:44:40 CST 2015 ===
===============================================================================
===============================================================================
= System overview =
===============================================================================
System type: RamSan-820
Version: 6.3.1-p10
Uptime: 07:44:46 up 248 days, 17:18, load average: 5.16, 5.26, 5.42
Capacity (GiB): 19199
Chassis serial: 00R1CC3
MTMS: 9834-AE2-00R1CC3
ETH0: 10.10.65.96
SID: ef275
=============================================================================== 

If Uptime is at 248 day, and box has halted…..Reboot 

 

Note: Due to the abrupt halt, and both interface cards halting simultaneously, recovery code can fail Flashcards that are not really not defective. Display using CLI “storage” command, or GUI storage panel. If Flashcards are in failed state, Contact Support for resolution. 

 

If lower than 248: 

1. Reboot box on a scheduled basis. Reboot will reset timer, and Uptime will be accurate. 

2. Use Cli, and reset Fibre Adapter cli command fc reset <controller> fc reset fc-1 or fc-2 wait a few minutes between adapter resets to let the network settle. Using this method is concurrent, but will not update Uptime counter 

For IB adapters: ib reset <controller> ib reset ib-1 or ib reset ib-2 

 

Fix Now Available: 

6.3.2-p2 posted on 04/06/2015 with resolution to 248-day bug
http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/ [http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/] 


On Flashsystem 840, or V840 

Firmware levels less than 1.1.3.7 are affected. 

All Adapter types are affected. FC, IB, FCOE, iSCSi 

Gui access: 

Login screen, or click on System: 

Top Left corner: 

[/support/docview.wss?uid=ssg1S1005126&aid=1] 

Click on Actions: 

[/support/docview.wss?uid=ssg1S1005126&aid=2] 

Click on Properties: 

[/support/docview.wss?uid=ssg1S1005126&aid=3] [/support/docview.wss?uid=ssg1S1005126&aid=3] 


We have found a more accurate way to Calculate Adapter level time remaining until Lockup:
(This may require support Assistance to complete)
On the System Report, search for os_cpu_tick


Total number of ticks for os_cpu_tick is 2147483648 


|===== OS Timers =====| -> sncon_cmd: os.timers
OS_Time == 542143780, 3 Active timers
[10000] ticks: 1 expire: 542143780 pdata:0x0 cb: os_cpu_tick
[10001] ticks: 30 expire: 542143807 pdata:0x0 cb: hw_dma_watchdog
[10002] ticks: 100 expire: 542143806 pdata:0x0 cb: sp_watchdog
Formula:

2147483648 - 542143780 / 100/ 60 / 60 /24 = 185 days remaining until Adapter lock-up

total ticks - current os_cpu_tick/100 /60 /60 /24 = days remaining until Adapter lockup


This must be repeated for ALL 4 Adapters 04, 08, 0C, and 10



Uptime is displayed… 

If at 248, and box has halted……..Reboot. 

stopsystem –reboot Note: This command is non-concurrent 

If below 248, three options: 



1. Upgrade to a new firmware level (if below 1.1.3.6). The action of installing firmware concurrently will reset the adapters one at a time. 


Fix Now Available: 

1.1.3.8-43.46 posted on 04/17/2015 with resolution to 248-day bug
http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/ [http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/] 

 

2. Schedule a reboot during a maintenance window. 

Note: Do not use GUI and do NOT use "satask stopnode to reboot. 

Use CLI procedure: 

1) Determine NON-config node using lsnodecanister
2) Reboot non-config node using stopsystem –reboot –node <node_name or id> (approximately 5 minutes)
3) force failover satask startservice -force THEN log back into cluster
(approximately 1 minute)
4) Reboot remaining node using stopsystem –reboot –node <node_name or id> (approximately 5 minutes)
3. Reset adapters one at a time using CLi commands: 

note: Using this method is concurrent, but will not update Uptime counter. 


svctask maintenance -action reset -canister 1 -adapter 1
svctask maintenance -action reset -canister 1 -adapter 2

(wait a few minutes to allow paths to recover)

svctask maintenance -action reset -canister 2 -adapter 1
svctask maintenance -action reset -canister 2 -adapter 2

question: I receive the following during UDX compilation Can't exec "/nz/kit/bin/adm/nzudxcompile": Argument list too long
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, the issue you are facing during UDX compilation is due to the ARG_MAX configuration parameter, which limits the number of arguments in a command line.

The key points from the context are:

1. The error message "Can't exec "/nz/kit/bin/adm/nzudxcompile": Argument list too long" indicates that the argument list passed to the nzudxcompile command is too long.

2. The ARG_MAX parameter on the system is set to 131072, which is the default value on older Red Hat 5.11 systems that the Twinfin platform supports.

3. Newer kernel versions (from 2.6.23 onwards) have increased the ARG_MAX value to 2621440, but the Twinfin platform is limited to the older Red Hat 5.11 version, which has the lower ARG_MAX value.

To resolve this issue, the recommended solution is:

1. There is no easy way to increase the ARG_MAX value on the Twinfin platform, as it is limited to the older Red Hat 5.11 version.

2. The workaround is to break down the UDX compilation process into smaller batches, so that the number of arguments passed to the nzudxcompile command does not exceed the ARG_MAX limit.

For example, you could split the compilation of multiple *.c files into smaller groups, and run the nzudxcompile command for each group separately. This should allow the command to execute successfully without hitting the ARG_MAX limit.

In summary, the issue is caused by the ARG_MAX configuration parameter, which is set to a lower value on the Twinfin platform. The resolution is to work around this limitation by breaking down the UDX compilation process into smaller, manageable steps.

reference_context:
TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

THIS DOCUMENT APPLIES ONLY TO THE FOLLOWING LANGUAGE VERSION(S):
 English 

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Following error can be met during UDX compilation.
Can't exec "/nz/kit/bin/adm/nzudxcompile": Argument list too long 

SYMPTOM
nzudxcompile command returns an "Argument list too long" error and aborts.


CAUSE
ARG_MAX configuration parameter limits the number of argument list in command line. In this case the client used more than 131072 argument with many program *.c files.

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
Check ARG_MAX value in the command line: 

$ getconf ARG_MAX
131072


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
This ARG_MAX kernel parameter adjusted to much larger from kernel release version 2.6.23, that is 2621440 (http://www.in-ulm.de/~mascheck/various/argmax [http://www.in-ulm.de/~mascheck/various/argmax]). 

But we support upto Redhat 5.11 in Twinfin which is less than kernel release 2.6.23. So we have no way to allow ARG_MAX larger than 131072 at this time.

question:
Problem with Postal database in Parascript  Hi

i am working with Parascript and i have installed successfully in my local system by folloing this guide [ Parascript FieldScript for Taskmaster Guide ]

here i can able to create vocabulary file, but storing multiple values into this the values are overridden and i do not understand how to create a postal database and access it

is that postal database is separate component ,,?? do we need to get somewhere and install ,,? they just mention in Guide the use of actions only

and does this parascript really helps to get better recognize.
ground_truth:
Based on the information provided in the TECHNOTE, the key points regarding the Postal database for Parascript FieldScript are:

1. The Postal database containing the United States Postal Service (USPS) address information is provided as a separate data disk that needs to be obtained through Passport Advantage.

2. The address information in the USPS database on the data disk is automatically disabled 105 days after the date of issue. IBM refreshes the data disk on the 14th day of every month, or the previous business day. Users must ensure they obtain the refreshed data from Passport Advantage on a regular schedule to avoid issues with disabled address information.

3. The Parascript FieldScript is used in conjunction with IBM Datacap Taskmaster Capture and is not a standalone component. The Postal database is meant to provide address validation and recognition capabilities within the Datacap Taskmaster Capture product.

4. There are no specific instructions in the provided guide about creating or accessing the Postal database directly. The guide focuses on installing and configuring the Parascript FieldScript component to work with Datacap Taskmaster Capture.

To answer your questions:

1. The Postal database is a separate component that needs to be obtained from Passport Advantage, along with the Parascript FieldScript installation.
2. Yes, you would need to install the Postal database component in addition to the Parascript FieldScript to utilize the address recognition capabilities.
3. The Parascript FieldScript is meant to provide improved recognition capabilities within the Datacap Taskmaster Capture product, but the effectiveness depends on the quality and freshness of the Postal database used.

I would recommend reaching out to IBM support or referring to the Datacap Taskmaster Capture documentation for more specific guidance on integrating the Postal database with Parascript FieldScript.

question:
recently installed Impact 7.1.0. fixpack 3, now when ever start the secondary impact server, the primary servers logging stops?  How can I fix this? when NCI1 is running by itself, all logging is functioning. When NCI2
is started, logging stops on NCI1 and the "reader" logs indicate that
the readers have stopped.
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, it appears that you are encountering an issue with the primary and secondary Impact servers in a cluster environment. When you start the secondary Impact server (NCI2), the logging on the primary Impact server (NCI1) stops, and the reader logs indicate that the readers have stopped.

Here are some steps you can take to try and resolve this issue:

1. **Check the PolicyList file**: The context mentions that a corrupted PolicyList file can cause issues with the cluster replication, leading to the secondary Impact server failing to start. Review the contents of the PolicyList file located at `$NCHOME/impact/etc/policylist` and ensure that it is not corrupted and has all the necessary entries and properties.

2. **Rebuild the PolicyList file**: If the PolicyList file is corrupted, you can try rebuilding it by following the steps provided in the "Resolving the Problem" section of the context:
   - Stop the Impact server using the `$NCHOME/bin/ewas.sh stop -user wasadmin -password netcool` command.
   - Rename the existing `$NCHOME/impact/etc/NCI1_policylist` file to `$NCHOME/impact/etc/NCI1_policylist.orig`.
   - Create a new `$NCHOME/impact/etc/NCI1_policylist` file.
   - Start the Impact server using the `$NCHOME/bin/ewas.sh start` command.

3. **Verify the cluster configuration**: Ensure that the cluster configuration is set up correctly, including the primary and secondary Impact servers. Check the cluster settings and ensure that the servers are properly communicating with each other.

4. **Check the Impact log files**: Review the Impact log files for any error messages or clues that might help identify the root cause of the issue. The log files are typically located in the `$NCHOME/impact/logs` directory.

5. **Apply the latest Fixpack**: Since you are using Impact 7.1.0 Fixpack 3, consider applying the latest available Fixpack for your Impact version. The latest Fixpack may include bug fixes or enhancements that could address the issue you are experiencing.

6. **Contact IBM Support**: If the above steps do not resolve the issue, you may need to contact IBM Support for further assistance. Provide them with the relevant log files and information about your Impact cluster configuration.

Remember to always follow the appropriate procedures and backup your data before making any changes to your Impact environment.

question: Why is MQ pattern showing installed MQ version as 8.0.0.2, instead of 8.0.0.4 when client tries to deploy pattern at the MQ 8.0.0.4 version in PureApplication System? In the pattern, it was clearly showing MQ version 8.0.0.4. After deployment, client checked the MQ version on the Virtual Machine and it shows 8.0.0.2 instead.
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, the issue seems to be related to a problem in the IBM Pure Application System when trying to install or migrate to IBM MQ 8.0.0.4. The key points are:

1. The IBM MQ Pure Application System pattern-types 1.0.0.2 and earlier have an issue where they can only install IBM MQ 8.0.0.2, even though the pattern-type may have a later version of IBM MQ (e.g., 8.0.0.4).

2. This is due to an interface change in IBM Pure Application System 2.1.2.0 and later versions, which prevents the installation and upgrade of IBM MQ to the latest maintenance level contained in the pattern-type.

3. The symptom is that the installation appears to succeed, but IBM MQ 8.0.0.2 is installed, and the subsequent fixpack update to 8.0.0.4 is not applied.

The resolution provided in the technote is to use a new maintenance.py lifecycle script, which can be obtained from IBM Fix Central. This script enables the installation and migration of the latest maintenance level of IBM MQ contained in the pattern-type, even on the affected IBM Pure Application System releases.

In summary, the mismatch between the expected IBM MQ version (8.0.0.4) and the actual installed version (8.0.0.2) is due to a known issue in the IBM MQ Pure Application System pattern-types 1.0.0.2 and earlier, which has been addressed by IBM with the new maintenance.py script.
all_answers:
Based on the provided context, the issue seems to be related to a problem in the IBM Pure Application System when trying to install or migrate to IBM MQ 8.0.0.4. The key points are:

1. The IBM MQ Pure Application System pattern-types 1.0.0.2 and earlier have an issue where they can only install IBM MQ 8.0.0.2, even though the pattern-type may have a later version of IBM MQ (e.g., 8.0.0.4).

2. This is due to an interface change in IBM Pure Application System 2.1.2.0 and later versions, which prevents the installation and upgrade of IBM MQ to the latest maintenance level contained in the pattern-type.

3. The symptom is that the installation appears to succeed, but IBM MQ 8.0.0.2 is installed, and the subsequent fixpack update to 8.0.0.4 is not applied.

The resolution provided in the technote is to use a new maintenance.py lifecycle script, which can be obtained from IBM Fix Central. This script enables the installation and migration of the latest maintenance level of IBM MQ contained in the pattern-type, even on the affected IBM Pure Application System releases.

In summary, the mismatch between the expected IBM MQ version (8.0.0.4) and the actual installed version (8.0.0.2) is due to a known issue in the IBM MQ Pure Application System pattern-types 1.0.0.2 and earlier, which has been addressed by IBM with the new maintenance.py script.
reference_context:
TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Users observe errors when attempting to install or migrate to IBM MQ 8.0.0.4 using IBM Pure Application System. 

SYMPTOM
Users observe errors when attempting to install or migrate to IBM MQ 8.0.0.4 using the following levels of IBM Pure Application System: 

 * 2.1.2.0 
 * 2.2.0.0 and later



This affects users with IBM MQ Pure Application System pattern-types 1.0.0.2 and earlier. 
CAUSE
Because of an interface change in IBM Pure Application System 2.1.2.0, and in 2.2.0.0 and greater, installation and upgrade of IBM MQ at the latest maintenance level contained in the pattern-type fails. The installation appears to succeed, but IBM MQ 8.0.0.2 is installed and the subsequent fixpack update is not applied. This affects IBM MQ Pure Application System pattern-types 1.0.0.2 and earlier, leading to the following restrictions: 

 * For IBM Pure Application System releases earlier than 2.1.2.0 and at version 2.1.2.1, you can install all versions of IBM MQ contained in the pattern-type. You can also apply maintenance to running pattern instances. 
 * For IBM Pure Application System 2.1.2.0, 2.2.0.0 and later, you can install IBM MQ 8.0.0.2 only. You cannot install later versions e.g., 8.0.0.4. You cannot apply maintenance to running pattern instances.

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
A new maintenance.py lifecycle script is available to enable installation and migration of the latest maintenance level of IBM MQ contained in the pattern-type. 


This update, and installation steps, can be obtained from IBM Fix Central:

http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/quickorder?parent=ibm%7EWebSphere&product=ibm/WebSphere/WebSphere+MQ&release=8.0&platform=All&function=fixId&fixids=1.0.0.2-WS-MQ-Pure-LAIT15252&includeSupersedes=0&source=fc [http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/quickorder?parent=ibm%7EWebSphere&product=ibm/WebSphere/WebSphere+MQ&release=8.0&platform=All&function=fixId&fixids=1.0.0.2-WS-MQ-Pure-LAIT15252&includeSupersedes=0&source=fc]


APAR IT15252 has been raised for the IBM MQ development team to address this issue within future releases of the IBM MQ Pure Application System pattern-type.

question:
How to resolve SessionBeanTimeoutException in WebSphere Application Server? In one of my j2EE-applications I get com.ibm.ejs.container.SessionBeanTimeoutExceptions with the following caused by section:
Caused by: com.ibm.ejs.container.SessionBeanTimeoutException: Stateful bean CMStatefulBeanO(BeanId(server1# mySessionBean.jar# StdEJBReader, A5667152-014B-4000-E000-9AD20A8E643B), state = METHOD_READY) timed out ... 26 more
Do you know how to resolve such SessionBeanTimeoutExceptions in general?
ground_truth:
To resolve the SessionBeanTimeoutException in WebSphere Application Server, you can try the following steps:

1. **Increase the session timeout value**:
   - Go to the WebSphere Integrated Solutions Console.
   - Navigate to "Application servers" > "server1" > "Session management" > "Session timeout".
   - Increase the timeout value to a larger number (e.g., 1800 seconds or 30 minutes).
   - Save the changes and restart the application server.

2. **Increase the EJB container's default session access timeout**:
   - Go to the WebSphere Integrated Solutions Console.
   - Navigate to "Application servers" > "server1" > "Process definition" > "Java Virtual Machine".
   - Click "Custom properties" and add a new property:
     - Name: `com.ibm.websphere.ejbcontainer.defaultSessionAccessTimeout`
     - Value: `30000` (30 seconds)
   - Save the changes and restart the application server.

3. **Increase the EJB container's default timeout values**:
   - Go to the WebSphere Integrated Solutions Console.
   - Navigate to "Application servers" > "server1" > "Enterprise JavaBeans" > "Container Settings".
   - Increase the following timeout values:
     - "Default access timeout": 300 seconds (5 minutes)
     - "Default remove timeout": 300 seconds (5 minutes)
     - "Default transaction timeout": 300 seconds (5 minutes)
   - Save the changes and restart the application server.

4. **Increase the transaction timeout value**:
   - Go to the WebSphere Integrated Solutions Console.
   - Navigate to "Application servers" > "server1" > "Transaction service".
   - Increase the "Transaction timeout" value to a larger number (e.g., 300 seconds or 5 minutes).
   - Save the changes and restart the application server.

5. **Check for long-running transactions or methods**:
   - Review your application code and identify any long-running transactions or methods that might be causing the session bean to time out.
   - Optimize the code or break down long-running tasks into smaller, more manageable operations.

6. **Enable session persistence**:
   - Go to the WebSphere Integrated Solutions Console.
   - Navigate to "Application servers" > "server1" > "Session management" > "Session persistence".
   - Enable session persistence and configure the appropriate settings.
   - Save the changes and restart the application server.

By trying these steps, you should be able to resolve the SessionBeanTimeoutException in your WebSphere Application Server environment.
all_answers:
To resolve the SessionBeanTimeoutException in WebSphere Application Server, you can try the following steps:

1. **Increase the session timeout value**:
   - Go to the WebSphere Integrated Solutions Console.
   - Navigate to "Application servers" > "server1" > "Session management" > "Session timeout".
   - Increase the timeout value to a larger number (e.g., 1800 seconds or 30 minutes).
   - Save the changes and restart the application server.

2. **Increase the EJB container's default session access timeout**:
   - Go to the WebSphere Integrated Solutions Console.
   - Navigate to "Application servers" > "server1" > "Process definition" > "Java Virtual Machine".
   - Click "Custom properties" and add a new property:
     - Name: `com.ibm.websphere.ejbcontainer.defaultSessionAccessTimeout`
     - Value: `30000` (30 seconds)
   - Save the changes and restart the application server.

3. **Increase the EJB container's default timeout values**:
   - Go to the WebSphere Integrated Solutions Console.
   - Navigate to "Application servers" > "server1" > "Enterprise JavaBeans" > "Container Settings".
   - Increase the following timeout values:
     - "Default access timeout": 300 seconds (5 minutes)
     - "Default remove timeout": 300 seconds (5 minutes)
     - "Default transaction timeout": 300 seconds (5 minutes)
   - Save the changes and restart the application server.

4. **Increase the transaction timeout value**:
   - Go to the WebSphere Integrated Solutions Console.
   - Navigate to "Application servers" > "server1" > "Transaction service".
   - Increase the "Transaction timeout" value to a larger number (e.g., 300 seconds or 5 minutes).
   - Save the changes and restart the application server.

5. **Check for long-running transactions or methods**:
   - Review your application code and identify any long-running transactions or methods that might be causing the session bean to time out.
   - Optimize the code or break down long-running tasks into smaller, more manageable operations.

6. **Enable session persistence**:
   - Go to the WebSphere Integrated Solutions Console.
   - Navigate to "Application servers" > "server1" > "Session management" > "Session persistence".
   - Enable session persistence and configure the appropriate settings.
   - Save the changes and restart the application server.

By trying these steps, you should be able to resolve the SessionBeanTimeoutException in your WebSphere Application Server environment.

question:
missing VM manager data 

we are missing VM manager data for 13 servers. error: invalid credentials - suspended. there are no recent changes done for the ID/pwd.

please suggest the action need to be taken.
1039699
ground_truth:
Based on the information provided, it seems there is an issue with the connection between the ILMT agent and the VM Manager (e.g., VMware vCenter). The error message "invalid credentials - suspended" suggests that the credentials used by the agent to access the VM Manager are no longer valid.

Here are the recommended steps to troubleshoot and resolve this issue:

1. **Verify the Credentials**:
   - Check the user ID and password being used by the ILMT agent to connect to the VM Manager.
   - Ensure that the credentials have not been changed or suspended on the VM Manager side.
   - Validate that the user account has the necessary permissions to access the required data.

2. **Restart the ILMT Agent**:
   - Try restarting the ILMT agent service/process to see if the connection can be re-established with the valid credentials.

3. **Check the ILMT Agent Configuration**:
   - Verify the VM Manager connection settings in the ILMT agent configuration, including the server address, port, and any other required parameters.
   - Ensure that the configuration is correct and matches the VM Manager environment.

4. **Test the Connection Manually**:
   - Try connecting to the VM Manager manually using the same credentials used by the ILMT agent.
   - This can help identify if the issue is specific to the ILMT agent or a more general connectivity problem.

5. **Review the ILMT Agent Logs**:
   - Check the ILMT agent logs for any additional error messages or clues that might help identify the root cause of the issue.
   - The logs may provide more information about the connection failure and any authentication-related errors.

6. **Coordinate with the VM Manager Team**:
   - If the issue persists, collaborate with the team responsible for managing the VM Manager environment.
   - They may be able to provide additional insights or assistance in resolving the credential-related problem.

7. **Consider Resetting the Credentials**:
   - If the existing credentials are indeed invalid or suspended, you may need to request new credentials from the VM Manager team.
   - Update the ILMT agent configuration with the new credentials and test the connection.

8. **Validate the Data Retrieval**:
   - Once the connection is re-established, verify that the ILMT agent is able to retrieve the missing VM manager data for the 13 servers.
   - Ensure that the data is being properly processed and reflected in the ILMT reports.

By following these steps, you should be able to identify and resolve the issue with the invalid credentials and retrieve the missing VM manager data.

Based on the information provided in the context, the following versions/levels of Java JRE and GSKit are included with each WebSphere MQ version:

- Java 5.0 Service Refresh 16 Fix Pack 8 (SR16-FP8)

- Java 6.0 Service Refresh 16 Fix Pack 2 (SR16-FP2)

- Java 7.0 Service Refresh 9 Fix Pack 32 (SR9-FP32) for HP-UX (IA32, IA64)

title: techqa

question: How to generate Javacores automatically in on hung threads reported in SystemOut.log for WebSphere Application Server? Need Javacores to be generated on hung threads messages reported in SystemOut.log for WebSphere Applicaiton Server.Example:WSVR0605W: Thread has been active for and may be hung. There are in total in the server that may be hung.
ground_truth:
Based on the information provided in the TECHNOTE, you can generate Javacores automatically for hung threads reported in the SystemOut.log for WebSphere Application Server by setting the following system property:

1. Set the system property `com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java` to `true`.

This will enable the automatic generation of Javacores for hung threads detected by the WebSphere Application Server thread monitor.

Additionally, you can control the frequency of Javacore generation by setting the `com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track` property:

- If `com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track` is not set, the default behavior is to generate a Javacore once per hung thread.
- If `com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track` is set to `true`, the thread monitor will generate Javacores continuously for as long as the thread remains hung.
- If `com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track` is set to `1`, the thread monitor will generate only one Javacore per hung thread.

To summarize, you can enable automatic Javacore generation for hung threads by setting the following system properties:

1. `com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java=true`
2. `com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track=1` (to generate only one Javacore per hung thread)

This will ensure that Javacores are automatically generated when hung threads are detected in the WebSphere Application Server SystemOut.log.

This will enable the automatic generation of Javacores for hung threads detected by the WebSphere Application Server thread monitor.

Additionally, you can control the frequency of Javacore generation by setting the `com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track` property:

- If `com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track` is not set, the default behavior is to generate a Javacore once per hung thread.
- If `com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track` is set to `true`, the thread monitor will generate Javacores continuously for as long as the thread remains hung.
- If `com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track` is set to `1`, the thread monitor will generate only one Javacore per hung thread.

To summarize, you can enable automatic Javacore generation for hung threads by setting the following system properties:

1. `com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java=true`
2. `com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track=1` (to generate only one Javacore per hung thread)

This will ensure that Javacores are automatically generated when hung threads are detected in the WebSphere Application Server SystemOut.log.

SUBSCRIBE
You can track all active APARs for this component.



APAR STATUS
 * CLOSED AS PROGRAM ERROR.
    
   
   

ERROR DESCRIPTION
 *  When com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java is set to true
   and if com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track is NOT
   set then by default a java thread dump is created continuously
   at the default hung thread detection time interval (default 3
   mins). So basically it will continuously produce java thread
   dumps every minute while the thread remains hung.
   
   In customer situation the hung thread never recovers and as a
   result a java thread dump is produced every 3 minutes and
   fills up gigabytes of storage.
   
   
    
   
   

LOCAL FIX
 *  You can set
   com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java to 1
   
   
    
   
   

PROBLEM SUMMARY
 *  ****************************************************************
   * USERS AFFECTED:  All users of IBM WebSphere Application      *
   *                  Server                                      *
   ****************************************************************
   * PROBLEM DESCRIPTION: The hung thread detection system        *
   *                      repeatedly dumps javacores for the      *
   *                      same hung thread                        *
   ****************************************************************
   * RECOMMENDATION:                                              *
   ****************************************************************
   The WebSphere Application Server thread monitor allows users
   to track hung threads by periodically dumping a javacore as
   long as the thread is detected as hung, using the
   com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track property.
   If this property is unset, the default value is set to be
   identical to the value of
   com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.  If the property is
   set to "true", the thread monitor sets those properties to the
   maximum integer value, so a thread hung for a long period of
   time would cause javacores to be thrown basically
   indefinitely.
   Users who had used this property on previous editions of
   WebSphere Application Server would have been likely to use the
   "true" value, as the property utilized boolean values in its
   original incarnation, but since the "track" property did not
   exist, they would have only had one javacore thrown per thread.
   
   
    
   
   

PROBLEM CONCLUSION
 *  The default value of the "track" property was modified so that
   if left unset, only one javacore would be thrown for a single
   hung thread.
   
   The fix for this APAR is currently targeted for inclusion in
   fix packs 8.5.5.12 and 9.0.0.3.  Please refer to the
   Recommended Updates page for delivery information:
   http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=180&uid=swg27004980 [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=180&uid=swg27004980]
   
   
    
   
   

TEMPORARY FIX
 *  As a workaround, users can set the system property
   com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track to "1".

title: techqa

question: Is there a scripting feature in ITM? Is there a scripting feature in ITM?
ground_truth:
Yes, there is a scripting feature in IBM Tivoli Monitoring (ITM). The context provided mentions the "Custom Scripting" feature in ITM, which allows users to execute custom scripts and reports the output data through special attribute groups.

Specifically, the context mentions the following:

- There are custom scripts that can be executed in ITM, and the data gathered from executing these scripts is reported using the KXX_Custom_Scripts_Runtime attribute groups (where XX stands for LZ, NT, or UX).
- However, the custom scripts implementation in ITM 6.30 FP5 used "pure event" tables, which meant situations could not receive "clearing" events and needed to be manually closed.
- To address this, new KXX_Custom_Scripts_Rtm_Smp "sampled" tables were added, which report the same custom script output data but in a way that allows situations to receive clearing events.

So in summary, yes, ITM does have a custom scripting feature that allows users to execute their own scripts and have the output data monitored and reported within the ITM environment.
all_answers:

- However, the custom scripts implementation in ITM 6.30 FP5 used "pure event" tables, which meant situations could not receive "clearing" events and needed to be manually closed.
- To address this, new KXX_Custom_Scripts_Rtm_Smp "sampled" tables were added, which report the same custom script output data but in a way that allows situations to receive clearing events.

So in summary, yes, ITM does have a custom scripting feature that allows users to execute their own scripts and have the output data monitored and reported within the ITM environment.

SUBSCRIBE TO THIS APAR
By subscribing, you receive periodic emails alerting you to the status of the APAR, along with a link to the fix after it becomes available. You can track this item individually or track all items by product.

Notify me when this APAR changes.

Notify me when an APAR for this component changes.



APAR STATUS
 * CLOSED AS PROGRAM ERROR.
    
   
   

ERROR DESCRIPTION
 *  Request for enhancement to the Custom Scripting feature as
   follows:
   
   one of my client was highly interested in this new feature but
   then very when he recognized, that this feature does only
   trigger pure. In fact most scripts do status monitoring with
   both, good and bad status for an item and therefore certainly
   need sampled events instead to see clearing events as well.
   
   Does this work as design, and if so, do we plan to change the
   behavior?
   
   Otherwise the agent will never achieve what it is supposed to:
   substitute hundreds of existing agentbuilder agents over time.
   
   
    
   
   

LOCAL FIX

PROBLEM SUMMARY
 *  Problem Description: Events from situations on custom scripts do
     not close automatically.
   
   Problem/Problem Summary: The data gathered executing the custom
    scripts in 6.30 FP5 are reported using the
    KXX_Custom_Scripts_Runtime attribute groups (where XX stands
    for LZ or NT or UX). These groups implement 'pure event'
    tables. Because of this, situations cannot receive
    'clearing' events and need to be manually closed.
   
   
    
   
   

PROBLEM CONCLUSION
 *  Fix/Problem Conclusion: New KXX_Custom_Scripts_Rtm_Smp sampled
    tables added. They report the same custom scripts' output data
    as those reported by the existing pure event tables.
   
    Specifications are as follow:
   
    Attribute group: KXX_Custom_Scripts_Rtm_Smp (multiple rows)
   
    Table: KXXSCRTSM (Sampled)
   
    Description: Data gathered using custom scripts. It contains
     the output rows of the scripts currently running.
   
    Attribute               Size Description
    ======================= ==== ==================================
    System_Name               32 This is the managed system name of
                                 the agent.
    Timestamp                 16 This is the local time when the
                                 data was collected
    Property_File            256 The name of the property file
    Attribute_Name            96 The attribute name that is defined
                                 in the properties file. The
                                 attribute is used for metric
                                 identification.
    Script_Path              512 The fully qualified path of the
                                 script.
    Custom_Name               96 The custom name that is defined
                                 in the properties file. It is used
                                 for custom reporting.
    Return_Code                4 Integer value returned by the
                                 script
    Row_Number                 4 Output row number.
    Standard_Output_Type       4 Standard output type of the script
    Standard_Output_String  2048 Script Standard Output in String
                                 format.
    Standard_Output_Integer    8 Script Output in Integer format
    Standard_Output_Float      8 Script Output in Floating Point
                                 format (2 decimals).
    Cust_Attr_Str1            64 Custom string attribute #1
    Cust_Attr_Str2            64 Custom string attribute #2
    Cust_Attr_Str3            64 Custom string attribute #3
    Cust_Attr_Str4            64 Custom string attribute #4
    Cust_Attr_Str5            64 Custom string attribute #5
    Cust_Attr_Int1             8 Custom integer attribute #1
    Cust_Attr_Int2             8 Custom integer attribute #2
    Cust_Attr_Int3             8 Custom integer attribute #3
    Cust_Attr_Int4             8 Custom integer attribute #4
    Cust_Attr_Int5             8 Custom integer attribute #5
    Cust_Attr_Float1           8 Custom floating point (2 decimals)
                                 attribute #1
    Cust_Attr_Float2           8 Custom floating point (2 decimals)
                                 attribute #2
    Cust_Attr_Float3           8 Custom floating point (2 decimals)
                                 attribute #3
    Cust_Attr_Float4           8 Custom floating point (2 decimals)
                                 attribute #4
    Cust_Attr_Float5           8 Custom floating point (2 decimals)
                                 attribute #5
   
    The OS agent sends events only if there are situations or
    historical collections using the pure event table; to
    completely avoid the sending of events, the Administrator can
    set to 'false' the environment variable:
   
    - KXX_FCP_SEND_SCRIPT_RUNTIME_EVENTS (default: true)
    If set to false, the OS agent stops sending events for each
    row of script standard output. In this case script outputs are
    visible on TEP console workspaces but no situations will be
    displayed and no historical collection data will be collected.
   
    No TEP workspace changes are introduced by this APAR, but a
    new built-in query named "Script execution results sampled",
    based on the new sampled tables, is provided.
   
   
   The fix for this APAR will be contained in the following
   maintenance packages:
   
   | FixPack    | 6.3.0-TIV-ITM-FP0007
   | InterimFix | 6.3.0.5-TIV-ITM_LINUX-IF0002
   | InterimFix | 6.3.0.5-TIV-ITM_UNIX-IF0002

question:
Can we create Match design specification db in xmeta Could you please let me know if we can create schema for Match design
specification database in xmeta db.
ground_truth:
Based on the information provided in the context, it is not recommended to create the Match Designer database within the XMETA database. The key points are:

1. The Match Designer database should be a separate database, not the XMETA database:

"You should start from an empty Database; using the default XMETA Database is not recommended because it can become corrupt."

2. The Match Designer database has specific requirements in terms of character set, page size, permissions, etc. that are different from the XMETA database requirements.

3. The Match Designer database is only used for development/testing purposes, not for production, so it should be kept separate from the production XMETA repository.

4. The context mentions that "there is nothing from this Database that is used in production runs so it should never be created on or moved to a production box."

So in summary, the recommendation is to create the Match Designer database as a separate database, not within the XMETA database. The XMETA database should be kept clean and separate from the Match Designer test/development environment.

reference_context:
MD Match Designer Matching Match InformationServer QualityStage TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 How do I configure the Match Designer Database in Information Server? 

ANSWER
This Technote outlines the following procedures. 

 1. Prerequisites 
 2. What is the Match Database’s purpose? 
 3. Setting the database up. 
 4. Configuring the Match Designer for use with the Database. 
 5. On going maintenance 


Prerequisites 
The following must exist before using the Match Designer functionality.  1. Match Specification built for use in match designer. 
 2. Create a data set that contains the data you will run the match analysis on. 
 3. Create a corresponding frequency data set to be used in the match analysis. 
 4. DSN for local PC 
 5. Create Database in which to perform the Match analysis processing. 
    a) Sizing of Database 
    b) Character sets required 
    c) Permission's for users 


Additional information is also available with in the WebSphere QualityStage User Guide. 

What it’s the Match Database’s purpose?  1. The results Database is used to test the match specifications (logic) designed for use in match jobs. 
 2. This functionality enables the user to get a baseline of how good the match criteria are, based on the match logic developed for use in the application. 
 3. The results Database stores statistics and match results based on your data. For Example: How many matches, duplicates, and unmatched records. 
 4. This functionality allows developers to change matching logic on the fly and retest within minutes. 

 * There is nothing from this Database that is used in production runs so it should never be created on or moved to a production box. 
 * The Match Designer is to be used for development purposes only with a "subset" of the full volume data. If additional match modifications are required continue to use a subset of data not conduct testing with full volume data. 
 * Determining the subset size of data to use in the Match Designer will vary depending on the clients environment and resources. Each client should run several iterations to determine the optimal subset to utilize in the Match Designer step. 
 * To obtain the most accurate matching thresholds a full volume run should be performed in the DataStage Designer.


Creating the Database  1. You should start from an empty Database; using the default XMETA Database is not recommended because it can become corrupt. 
 2. There are certain requirements on how the Database is to be created. 
    a) If Oracle: CHARACTERSET should be set to AL32UTF 
    b) If SQL: default is fine. 
    c) If DB2: create as codepage UTF-8 and page size 32K 
    d) Permission’s - Each user has to have the ability to create tables, write and read rights in the DB. 
    e) The minimum DB space required will be calculated as follows = Number of Passes in match specification (X) Number of Input Records (X) Input Record Length (+) 100 filler.
    f) If this is a distributed installation, you need to have ODBC defined in your client and on the server. They must have the same name. 


Configuring the Match Designer for use with the Database  1.  Via the DataStage Designer navigate to the your match specification to work with 
 2.  Double click and a window will appear indicating: 
     “The ODBC Data Source Name (xxxxxxx) currently specified for this Match could not be found. Please create a DSN by this name or run a Test Environment Update to configure a different DSN for this Specification.” 
 3.  Select the “OK” button 
 4.  Select option “Configure Specification” 
 5.  From drop down select “Test Environment” 
 6.  For “Sample Information” navigate to the location of your data set(s) created for analysis. 
 7.  Under “Frequency Information” navigate to the location of your frequency data set(s) created for use. 
 8.  Section “Test Results Database” fill the following information in. 
     a. Select the DSN created for the will allow the connection to the Database. 
     b. Enter user name required 
     c. Password required 
     d. Test Connection 
     e. Configuration File Location; these are the nodes located in your environment for use. Please make sure this matches the location of where the data sets reside on the server. 
 9.  Once connection is established hit the “Update” button. This will populate the Database with the required fields (not data) to populate when you perform the test specification. 
 10. The final step is to perform the Test All Passes. Please note this make take a few moments depending on the size of the files used. 


Ongoing Maintenance  1. For each new run, results are cleared and rewritten to the DB. 
 2. Each user should have their own respective Databases to avoid rewriting over previous results. 
 3. There are separate tables holding results for each design. And from one user it is no problem to use a single Database and multiple designs. 
 4. Per the recommendation above in "Creating the Database" this database should not be your XMETA database. If you used the XMETA DO NOT RUN THIS STEP. When you are done testing you can drop the DB altogether.

question:
ORA-01795 Error accessing Decision Center Business Console 8.7 I am working with ODM 8.7.0.0 when accessing the Business Console I am getting the below error message :
ORA-01795: maximum number of expressions in a list is 1000
The diagnostics do not contain any warning nor error.
The logs contain below exception : 
 java.lang.RuntimeException: java.sql.SQLException: ORA-01795: maximum number of expressions in a list is 1000
...
 Caused by: java.sql.SQLException: ORA-01795: maximum number of expressions in a list is 1000
ground_truth:
Based on the information provided in the context, it seems that the issue you are facing with the ORA-01795 error when accessing the Decision Center Business Console in ODM 8.7.0.0 is similar to the issues described in the APAR information.

The key points from the context are:

1. The ORA-01795 error occurs when there are more than 1000 attributes or merchandising associations per catalog entry in the WebSphere Commerce Management Center.

2. The issue was addressed by APAR LI74800 for WebSphere Commerce V6.0 and APAR JR50396 for WebSphere Commerce V7.0.

3. The root cause was that the SQL in the data service layer was trying to handle more than 1000 entries, which exceeded the Oracle limit.

4. The fix involved modifying the SQL to handle the scenario when the catalog entry IDs exceed the Oracle 1000 max parameter limitation.

Given that your issue is with the Decision Center Business Console in ODM 8.7.0.0, and the error message is similar, it's likely that you are hitting a similar issue with the underlying SQL queries exceeding the Oracle 1000 expression limit.

The recommended course of action would be:

1. Check if there is an available fix or APAR for the specific version of ODM you are using (8.7.0.0) that addresses this issue.

2. If no fix is available, you can try the local fix mentioned in the context, which is to run a full preprocess/buildindex rather than a delta index when the number of entries exceeds 1000.

3. Consult with IBM support to see if they can provide a more specific solution for your ODM 8.7.0.0 environment.

The key is to identify and address the root cause of the SQL query exceeding the Oracle expression limit, which seems to be the underlying issue based on the information provided.

question:
Error: "MBEANSTARTER LOADEXTENSIONS FAILED TO LOAD EXTENSION" occurs at portal startup We are using Portal Server 8.5 and when starting the Portal server we see following error:

0000005e MBeanStarter E com.ibm.wps.scripting.server.MBeanStarter loadExtensions failed to load extension: key: ServerExt.applications / value: com.ibm.wps.scripting.server.ApplicationServerExtensions

How can we resolve this error?
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, the error "MBEANSTARTER LOADEXTENSIONS FAILED TO LOAD EXTENSION" occurs during the startup of IBM WebSphere Portal 8.5.0.0. The error message indicates that the MBeanStarter failed to load the extension with the key "ServerExt.applications" and the value "com.ibm.wps.scripting.server.ApplicationServerExtensions".

To resolve this issue, the following steps can be taken:

1. Open the WebSphere Application Server (WAS) administrative console.
2. Navigate to Resources > Resource environment providers > WP ScriptingService > Custom properties.
3. Locate the entry with the name "ServerExt.applications" and the value "com.ibm.wps.scripting.server.ApplicationServerExtensions".
4. Delete this entry and save the changes.
5. Restart the server.

The context provided mentions that this issue was addressed by a code fix, which was integrated into the WebSphere Portal & WCM Combined Cumulative Fix 06 (PI36060) for Portal 8.5.0.0. So, if you are experiencing this issue, you can apply the cumulative fix to resolve the problem.

The key steps are:

1. Delete the outdated resource environment provider entry from the WAS administrative console.
2. If the issue persists, apply the cumulative fix PI36060 from Fix Central.

By following these steps, you should be able to resolve the "MBEANSTARTER LOADEXTENSIONS FAILED TO LOAD EXTENSION" error during Portal 8.5.0.0 startup.

question:
IBM Integration Designer could not connect to the DataPower appliance. I am using WDP XI52 with Firmware 7.0.0.7 and IID 7.5.1. When I try to connect WDP from [DataPower Appliances] View in IID, I got a error message as follows : "SSL handshake on the connection to the DataPower appliance "x.x.x.x" was not successful. Contact the DataPower administrator." This situations occurred since WDP Firmware had upgraded from 7.0.0.2 to 7.0.0.5. (Now it was upgraded to 7.0.0.7)
Could you please let me know what I should check to solve this problem? Please note that this WDP works normally and a network is normal. Also, I can connect it by using WebGUI and SSH terminal. And I've checked that IP , XML Management Interface Port, Web Management Service Port and account/password are correct.
ground_truth:
Based on the information provided, it seems that the issue is related to the SSL/TLS configuration between the IBM Integration Designer (IID) and the DataPower appliance. Here are some steps you can take to troubleshoot and resolve the problem:

1. **Check the SSL/TLS configuration on the DataPower appliance**:
   - Ensure that the SSL/TLS configuration on the DataPower appliance is up-to-date and secure.
   - Verify that the DataPower appliance is configured to use TLS 1.1 or TLS 1.2 and that older versions of SSL/TLS (e.g., SSL 3.0, TLS 1.0) are disabled.
   - Ensure that the DataPower appliance is not vulnerable to the BEAST (Browser Exploit Against SSL/TLS) attack by enabling the appropriate mitigations.

2. **Verify the SSL/TLS configuration in IBM Integration Designer (IID)**:
   - In IID, go to the "DataPower Appliances" view and select the DataPower appliance.
   - Right-click on the appliance and select "Properties".
   - In the "Properties" window, go to the "SSL/TLS" tab and ensure that the settings match the configuration on the DataPower appliance.
   - Try connecting to the DataPower appliance again.

3. **Check the SSL/TLS cipher suites**:
   - Ensure that the SSL/TLS cipher suites configured on the DataPower appliance are compatible with the ones supported by IID.
   - You can check the supported cipher suites in IID by going to "Window" > "Preferences" > "WebSphere DataPower" > "SSL/TLS Cipher Suites".

4. **Verify the SSL/TLS certificates**:
   - Ensure that the SSL/TLS certificates used by the DataPower appliance are valid and trusted by IID.
   - If necessary, import the DataPower appliance's SSL/TLS certificate into the trust store used by IID.

5. **Check the network connectivity**:
   - Ensure that the network connection between IID and the DataPower appliance is stable and that there are no firewall or network-related issues.
   - Try connecting to the DataPower appliance from a different network or device to rule out any local network problems.

6. **Update the firmware and IBM Integration Designer (IID)**:
   - Consider upgrading the DataPower appliance firmware to the latest version, as newer versions may include bug fixes or improvements related to SSL/TLS connectivity.
   - Ensure that you are using the latest version of IBM Integration Designer (IID) that is compatible with the DataPower appliance firmware version.

If the issue persists after trying these steps, you may need to contact IBM Support for further assistance in troubleshooting the SSL/TLS connectivity between the IBM Integration Designer and the DataPower appliance.

reference_context:
SSL ; TLS/SSL; TLS 1.0; TLS 1.1; BEAST; Vulnerability; CVE-2011-3389 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 A known SSL/TLS vulnerability, CVE-2011-3389 (also known as BEAST), exists in the SSLv3 and TLS 1.0 protocols. To eliminate this vulnerability, please review the specific configuration recommendations below. 

SYMPTOM
Refer to CVE-2011-3389


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
The following describes how to prevent the CVE-2011-3389 vulnerability on any firmware version of the IBM WebSphere DataPower appliance.

The protocol problem in this case is the use of a predictable IV (initialization vector) when block cipher CBC mode is used. There are two ways to prevent the attack.

1. use TLS 1.1 or TLS 1.2 

The protocol problem that allows the attack was fixed completely starting in TLS 1.1 which changed the spec to use a new, random IV in each encrypted SSL record. 

DataPower firmware release 6.0.0 added support for TLS 1.1 or TLS 1.2 by default. To protect the appliance against the vulnerability enable support for TLS 1.1 and TLS 1.2 in the Crypto Profile while disabling support for TLS 1.0, SSLv3, and SSLv2.

DataPower firmware releases prior to 6.0.0 do not support TLS 1.1 or TLS 1.2 and therefore must use option #2 below to avoid the vulnerability.


2. Enable the empty data record counter measure (CLI only)

This counter measure can only be enabled using the command line interface (it is not available in the WebGUI).

This counter measure is to send empty data records between the usual data records to effectively randomize the IV value. This behavior is legal according to the SSL/TLS specifications, but some SSL/TLS stacks are not fully compliant to this specification and will reject connections that send such records. Therefore use of this setting may result in incompatibility with SSL/TLS stacks that are not standards compliant such as the one in some versions of Microsoft Internet Explorer (IE).

The Crypto Profile has a property called Options. Using the command line interface, set the Options field by entering crypto mode and using the profile command with an options mask of 0x10007FF: 

profile name idCred [ssl name] [ciphers cipher-string] [options mask] 

For example, a Crypto Profile named "alice-cp" uses an Identification Credential named "alice-idcred". The following CLI commands will enable the empty data record counter measure for this Crypto Profile:

top
configure terminal
crypto
profile alice-cp alice-idcred options 0x10007FF

This counter measure only protects one half of the duplex (outgoing data from DataPower). It is up to the SSL peer to protect the other half of the duplex with the same counter measure. If this is undesirable then use #1 or #3 instead. 


3. Use SSL Server Profile or SSL SNI Server Profile with firmware version 7.2.0 or later

In 7.2.0 release, IBM DataPower Gateways introduced SSL Server Profile and SSL SNI Server Profile. These 2 profiles are not vulnerable to this advisory, as they will always send empty data records between the usual data records.

question: Help with vulnerability have been identified in WebSphere Application Server shipped with WSRR I need to understand details regarding Multiple vulnerabilities have been identified in WebSphere Application Server shipped with WebSphere Service Registry and Repository (CVE-2017-1583). Where can I find this information?
ground_truth:
Based on the provided security bulletins, the following information is available regarding the vulnerabilities in WebSphere Application Server (WAS) shipped with WebSphere Service Registry and Repository (WSRR):

1. The security bulletin titled "Multiple vulnerabilities affect Java Server Faces (JSF) used by WebSphere Application Server" (http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22008707) provides details on the vulnerabilities CVE-2017-1583 and CVE-2011-4343 that affect JSF used by WAS.

2. This bulletin states that the issues are addressed by the supported versions of WAS that are shipped with WSRR:

   - WSRR V8.5 is affected and the issues are addressed by WAS V8.5.5.
   - WSRR V8.0 is affected and the issues are addressed by WAS V8.0.

3. Customers should refer to this security bulletin for vulnerability details and information about the applicable fixes for the WAS versions shipped with WSRR.

So in summary, the details on the vulnerabilities affecting WAS shipped with WSRR can be found in the security bulletin titled "Multiple vulnerabilities affect Java Server Faces (JSF) used by WebSphere Application Server".

question:
startNode failed with ADML3000E after a deployment failure Client had a deployment failure due to "Too many open files" error.

Afterwards, the startNode failed with ADML3000E:

What could cause the "Too many open files error?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, the "Too many open files" error is likely caused by a file descriptor or socket leak in the application or the application server environment.

The key points from the context are:

1. The deployment failed with a "Too many open files" error, which indicates that the process exhausted the available file handles.

2. After this failure, the startNode command failed with the ADML3000E error, which is likely a result of the previous deployment failure and the lingering open file handles.

The "Too many open files" error can be caused by the following:

1. The application code or libraries are not properly closing/releasing file handles or sockets after use. This can lead to a gradual increase in the number of open files until the limit is reached.

2. The system-level file handle limit (ulimit) is set too low for the application's requirements. Increasing the ulimit can help mitigate this issue.

3. Issues with the application server configuration, such as insufficient resources or improper settings, can also lead to file handle exhaustion.

To resolve the issue, the recommended steps would be:

1. Investigate the application code and libraries to identify any potential file handle or socket leaks.
2. Check the system-level file handle limit (ulimit) and increase it if necessary.
3. Review the application server configuration and ensure that the settings are appropriate for the application's requirements.
4. Restart the application server to release any lingering open file handles or sockets.
5. Redeploy the application after resolving the underlying issue to prevent the "Too many open files" error from occurring again.

By addressing the root cause of the "Too many open files" error, you can ensure that the startNode command can execute successfully without encountering the ADML3000E error.
all_answers:
Based on the context provided, the "Too many open files" error is likely caused by a file descriptor or socket leak in the application or the application server environment.

The key points from the context are:

1. The deployment failed with a "Too many open files" error, which indicates that the process exhausted the available file handles.

2. After this failure, the startNode command failed with the ADML3000E error, which is likely a result of the previous deployment failure and the lingering open file handles.

The "Too many open files" error can be caused by the following:

1. The application code or libraries are not properly closing/releasing file handles or sockets after use. This can lead to a gradual increase in the number of open files until the limit is reached.

2. The system-level file handle limit (ulimit) is set too low for the application's requirements. Increasing the ulimit can help mitigate this issue.

3. Issues with the application server configuration, such as insufficient resources or improper settings, can also lead to file handle exhaustion.

To resolve the issue, the recommended steps would be:

1. Investigate the application code and libraries to identify any potential file handle or socket leaks.
2. Check the system-level file handle limit (ulimit) and increase it if necessary.
3. Review the application server configuration and ensure that the settings are appropriate for the application's requirements.
4. Restart the application server to release any lingering open file handles or sockets.
5. Redeploy the application after resolving the underlying issue to prevent the "Too many open files" error from occurring again.

By addressing the root cause of the "Too many open files" error, you can ensure that the startNode command can execute successfully without encountering the ADML3000E error.
reference_context:
wasrun; wasjdk; open; files; too; ProblemDeterminationDocument; JCC was appserver app server TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 This technote explains how to debug the "Too many open files" error message on Microsoft Windows, AIX, Linux and Solaris operating systems. 

SYMPTOM
The following messages could be displayed when the process has exhausted the file handle limit: 

java.io.IOException: Too many open files

[3/14/15 9:26:53:589 EDT] 14142136 prefs W Could not lock User prefs. Unix error code 24.

New sockets/file descriptors can not be opened after the limit has been reached. 


CAUSE
System configuration limitation.

When the "Too Many Open Files" error message is written to the logs, it indicates that all available file handles for the process have been used (this includes sockets as well). In a majority of cases, this is the result of file handles being leaked by some part of the application. This technote explains how to collect output that identifies what file handles are in use at the time of the error condition.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM




Determine Ulimits

On UNIX and Linux operating systems, the ulimit for the number of file handles can be configured, and it is usually set too low by default. Increasing this ulimit to 8000 is usually sufficient for normal runtime, but this depends on your applications and your file/socket usage. Additionally, file descriptor leaks can still occur even with a high value. 

Display the current soft limit:
ulimit -Sn

Display the current hard limit:
ulimit -Hn

Or capture a Javacore, the limit will be listed in that file under the name NOFILE:
kill -3 PID


Please see the following document if you would like more information on where you can edit ulimits:
Guidelines for setting ulimits (WebSphere Application Server)
http://www.IBM.com/support/docview.wss?rs=180&uid=swg21469413 [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=180&uid=swg21469413]


Operating Systems 

Windows By default, Windows does not ship with a tool to debug this type of problem. Instead Microsoft provides a tool that you can download called Process Explorer [http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/bb896653]. This tool identifies the open handles/files associated with the Java™ process (but usually not sockets opened by the Winsock component) and determines which handles are still opened. These handles result in the "Too many open files" error message. 

To display the handles, click on the Gear Icon [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21067352&aid=1] in the toolbar (or press CTRL+ H to toggle the handles view). The icon will change to the icon for DLL files (so you can toggle it back to the DLL view). 

It is important that you change the Refresh Rate. Select View > Update Speed, and change it to 5 seconds. 

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21067352&aid=2] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21067352&aid=2] 

There is also another Microsoft utility called Handle that you can download from the following URL: 
https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/bb896655.aspx [https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/bb896655.aspx] 

This tool is a command line version of Process Explorer. The URL above contains the usage instructions. 


AIX 
The commands lsof and procfiles are usually the best commands to determine what files and sockets are opened.. 

lsof 
To determine if the number of open files is growing over a period of time, issue lsof to report the open files against a PID on a periodic basis. For example: 

lsof -p [PID] -r [interval in seconds, 1800 for 30 minutes] > lsof.out 

This output does not give the actual file names to which the handles are open. It provides only the name of the file system (directory) in which they are contained. The lsof command indicates if the open file is associated with an open socket or a file. When it references a file, it identifies the file system and the inode, not the file name. 

It is best to capture lsof several times to see the rate of growth in the file descriptors. 

procfiles 
The procfiles command does provide similar information, and also displays the full filenames loaded. It may not show sockets in use. 

procfiles -n [PID] > procfiles.out 


Other commands (to display filenames that are opened) 

INODES and DF 
df -kP filesystem_from_lsof | awk '{print $6}' | tail -1 
>> Note the filesystem name 
find filesystem_name -inum inode_from_lsof -print > filelist.out 
>> Shows the actual file name 

svmon 
svmon -P PID -m | grep pers (for JFS) 
svmon -P PID -m | grep clnt (for JFS2, NFS) 
(this opens files in the format: filesystem_device:inode) 

Use the same procedure as above for finding the actual file name. 


Linux To determine if the number of open files is growing over a period of time, issue lsof to report the open files against a PID on a periodic basis. For example: 

lsof -p [PID] -r [interval in seconds, 1800 for 30 minutes] > lsof.out 

The output will provide you with all of the open files for the specified PID. You will be able to determine which files are opened and which files are growing over time. 

It is best to capture lsof several times to see the rate of growth in the file descriptors. 


Alternately you can list the contents of the file descriptors as a list of symbolic links in the following directory, where you replace PID with the process ID. This is especially useful if you don't have access to the lsof command: 

ls -al /proc/PID/fd 


Solaris Run the following commands to monitor open file (socket) descriptors on Solaris: 

pfiles 
/usr/proc/bin/pfiles [ PID ] > pfiles.out 

lsof 
lsof -p [ PID ] > lsof.ou t 

This will get one round of lsof output. If you want to determine if the number of open files is growing over time, you can issue the command with the -r option to capture multiple intervals: 
lsof -p [PID] -r [interval in seconds, 1800 for 30 minutes] > lsof.out 

It is best to capture lsof several times to see the rate of growth in the file descriptors. 

HP-UX 
lsof 
lsof -p [ PID ] > lsof.out 

This will get one round of lsof output. If you want to determine if the number of open files is growing over time, you can issue the command with the -r option to capture multiple intervals: 
lsof -p [PID] -r [interval in seconds, 1800 for 30 minutes] > lsof.out 

It is best to capture lsof several times to see the rate of growth in the file descriptors.

SUMMARY
 A vulnerability in Apache Commons FileUpload was addressed by IBM InfoSphere Information Server. 

VULNERABILITY DETAILS

CVEID: CVE-2016-1000031 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2016-1000031]
DESCRIPTION: Apache Commons FileUpload, as used in IBM InfoSphere Information Server, could allow a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code on the system, caused by deserialization of untrusted data in DiskFileItem class of the FileUpload library. A remote attacker could exploit this vulnerability to execute arbitrary code under the context of the current process.
CVSS Base Score: 9.8
CVSS Temporal Score: See https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/117957 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/117957] for the current score

question:
How do I configure TLS/SSL for an IIB web service provider and consumer? I'm investigating ways and technologies for use with IIB to run a web service that runs as both a provider and consumer. I'm looking at HTTP and SOAP messages/nodes. At a high level it might look like this:
HTTPInput (TLS over HTTPS) -> Compute -> HTTPRequest (SSL over HTTPS) -> Compute -> HTTPReply
or
SOAPInput (TLS over HTTPS) -> Compute -> SOAPRequest (SSL over HTTPS) -> Compute -> SOAPReply
Does anyone have more details/references beyond the knowledge center on how to configure the TLS/SSL portions?
ground_truth:
To configure TLS/SSL for an IIB web service provider and consumer, you can follow these general steps:

1. **Configure SSL/TLS for the HTTP Nodes**:
   - For the HTTPInput node (web service provider), configure it to use HTTPS by setting the "SSL/TLS Configuration" property to the appropriate SSL/TLS configuration.
   - For the HTTPRequest node (web service consumer), configure it to use HTTPS by setting the "SSL/TLS Configuration" property to the appropriate SSL/TLS configuration.

2. **Configure the SSL/TLS Configuration**:
   - In the IIB toolkit, go to the "Configurations" section and create a new SSL/TLS configuration.
   - In the SSL/TLS configuration, specify the following:
     - Key repository file: The location of the keystore file containing the SSL/TLS certificates.
     - Key repository password: The password for the keystore.
     - Trust repository file: The location of the truststore file containing the trusted SSL/TLS certificates.
     - Trust repository password: The password for the truststore.
     - SSL/TLS protocol version: The version of the SSL/TLS protocol to use (e.g., TLS 1.2).
     - Cipher specifications: The cipher suites to use for the SSL/TLS connections.

3. **Configure the SSL/TLS Certificates**:
   - Obtain the necessary SSL/TLS certificates (server and client certificates) and import them into the appropriate key and trust repositories.
   - Ensure that the certificates are valid and trusted by the web service provider and consumer.

4. **Configure the HTTP Nodes**:
   - For the HTTPInput node (web service provider), set the "SSL/TLS Configuration" property to the SSL/TLS configuration created in step 2.
   - For the HTTPRequest node (web service consumer), set the "SSL/TLS Configuration" property to the SSL/TLS configuration created in step 2.

5. **Configure the SOAP Nodes (if using SOAP)**:
   - For the SOAPInput node (SOAP web service provider), set the "SSL/TLS Configuration" property to the SSL/TLS configuration created in step 2.
   - For the SOAPRequest node (SOAP web service consumer), set the "SSL/TLS Configuration" property to the SSL/TLS configuration created in step 2.

6. **Test the SSL/TLS Configuration**:
   - Test the web service provider and consumer by sending requests over the HTTPS/SSL-enabled channels.
   - Verify that the SSL/TLS connection is established successfully and that the data is encrypted.

Here are some additional resources that provide more details on configuring TLS/SSL for IIB:

- IBM Integration Bus Knowledge Center: [Configuring SSL/TLS for HTTP and SOAP nodes](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/integration-bus/10.0?topic=nodes-configuring-ssltls-http-soap)
- IBM Integration Bus Knowledge Center: [Configuring SSL/TLS for Integration Servers](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/integration-bus/10.0?topic=servers-configuring-ssltls-integration)
- IBM Integration Bus Security Concepts: [Securing connections using SSL/TLS](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/integration-bus/10.0?topic=security-securing-connections-using-ssltls)

Remember to ensure that the SSL/TLS configuration, certificates, and cipher suites are aligned with your organization's security policies and best practices.
