reference_context:
TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 If you have an IBM i Apache HTTP Server configured for SSL that is associated with an IBM WebSphere Application Server v8.5 or later profile, an HTTP 500 Internal Server Error might occur when accessing your web application. 

SYMPTOM
HTTP 500 Internal Server Error received in your web browser when accessing your web application URL 

AND 

The following errors appear in the plugins_root/logs/web_server_name/http_plugin.log file. 


ERROR: ws_common: websphereFindTransport: Nosecure transports available
ERROR: ws_common: websphereWriteRequestReadResponse: Failed to find a transport
ERROR: ESI: getResponse: failed to get response: rc = 4
ERROR: ws_common: websphereHandleRequest: Failed to handle request


CAUSE
Beginning at IBM WebSphere Application Server v8.5.5, the WebSphere Web Server Plugin product is no longer redirecting HTTPS SSL communications to the HTTP IP transport if the WebSphere Web Server Plugin is not properly configured to accept SSL communications.


ENVIRONMENT
IBM i; IBM WebSphere Application Server v8.5.5 and later



DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
Verify a HTTP 500 Internal Server Error is received in the web browser when accessing the web application's URL. Check the plugins_root/logs/web_server_name/http_plugin.log file for errors. The http_plugin.log file is typically located in the /QIBM/UserData/WebSphere/AppServer/<version>/<edition>/profiles/<profileName>/logs/<IHS_serverName>/ directory.



RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Beginning at IBM WebSphere Application Server v8.5.5, the WebSphere Web Server Plugin product is no longer redirecting HTTPS SSL communications to the HTTP IP transport if the WebSphere Web Server Plugin is not properly configured to accept SSL communications. Because of this, you will receive the following errors in the plugins_root/logs/web_server_name/http_plugin.log file if the Web Server plugin is not properly configured to accept SSL communications. 


The following messages indicate the Web Server plugin's key database file has not been copied to the web server keystore directory. Thus, the secure HTTPS transport cannot be initialized.

ERROR: lib_security: logSSLError: str_security (gsk error 202): Key database file was not found.
ERROR: lib_security: initializeSecurity: Failed to initialize GSK environment. Secure transports are not possible.
ERROR: ws_transport: transportInitializeSecurity: Failed to initialize security. Secure transports are not possible.
ERROR: ws_server: serverAddTransport: Failed to initialize security. Secure transports are not possible.
ERROR: ws_server: serverAddTransport: HTTPS Transport is skipped. IMPORTANT: If a HTTP transport is defined, it will be used for communication to the application server.
ERROR: ws_server: serverAddTransport: Plugin will continue to startup, however, SSL transport PMICI7.PNAT.COM:6003 did not initilize. Secure communication between app server and plugin will NOT occur. To run with SSL, additional products may need to be installed: 1) OS/400 Digital Certificate Manager (5722-SS1 or 5769-SS1, option 34) 2) Cryptographic Access Provider 5769-AC1 (40-bit), 5722-AC2 or 5769-AC2 (56-bit), 5722-AC3 or 5769-AC3 (128-bit)
...

The following messages indicate no active secure HTTPS transport can be found. This is a direct result of the previous messages.

ERROR: ws_common: websphereFindTransport: Nosecure transports available
ERROR: ws_common: websphereWriteRequestReadResponse: Failed to find a transport
ERROR: ESI: getResponse: failed to get response: rc = 4
ERROR: ws_common: websphereHandleRequest: Failed to handle request

To resolve your issue, IBM recommends the following steps be taken to enable the Web Server plugin to accept SSL/TLS communications.

The steps to configure the web server plugin to accept SSL communications are listed here: 
NOTE:You can ignore step 1 since it is not needed on the IBM i. 
http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/wasinfo/v8r5/topic/com.ibm.websphere.express.doc/ae/tsec_httpserv2.html [http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/wasinfo/v8r5/topic/com.ibm.websphere.express.doc/ae/tsec_httpserv2.html]

Once the web server plugin is properly configured for SSL, restart your Apache HTTP Server and review the http_plugin.log file to confirm the following messages no longer appear in the log.

ERROR: lib_security: logSSLError: str_security (gsk error 202): Key database file was not found.
ERROR: lib_security: initializeSecurity: Failed to initialize GSK environment. Secure transports are not possible.
ERROR: ws_transport: transportInitializeSecurity: Failed to initialize security. Secure transports are not possible.
ERROR: ws_server: serverAddTransport: Failed to initialize security. Secure transports are not possible.
ERROR: ws_server: serverAddTransport: HTTPS Transport is skipped. IMPORTANT: If a HTTP transport is defined, it will be used for communication to the application server.
ERROR: ws_server: serverAddTransport: Plugin will continue to startup, however, SSL transport PMICI7.PNAT.COM:6003 did not initilize. Secure communication between app server and plugin will NOT occur. To run with SSL, additional products may need to be installed: 1) OS/400 Digital Certificate Manager (5722-SS1 or 5769-SS1, option 34) 2) Cryptographic Access Provider 5769-AC1 (40-bit), 5722-AC2 or 5769-AC2 (56-bit), 5722-AC3 or 5769-AC3 (128-bit)

================================================================

If the Web Server plug-in key database (plugin-key.kdb) does not exist in the location specified in the plugin-cfg.xml file being used by the IBM HTTP Server, then you might be able to implement the "UseInsecure=true" custom plug-in property to your web server definition to resolve your issue. In some cases when the web server is partially configured for SSL/TLS communications, the "UseInsecure=true" custom property will be ignored. In this case, you would need to disable the HTTPS transport for the Application Server to use non-SSL between the plugin and the application server. IBM strongly does not recommend disabling the HTTPS transport for your application server since this would prevent all secure HTTPS connections to your application server.

NOTE: This process allows non-secure communications between the Web Server Plug-in and the WebSphere Application Server. If you would like these communications to be secure, please refer to the recommendation above on how to "Configure the Web Server plugin to accept SSL/TLS communications".

=================================================================

Implement the "UseInsecure=true" custom plug-in property for your web server definition.

You can implement the "UseInsecure-true" custom plug-in property to allow HTTPS traffic to be redirected to the HTTP transport. This enables WAS to function like it did at WAS v8.0 and earlier. If you want to allow the Web Server plugin to create non-secure connections when secure connections are defined (the old behavior), you will need to create the custom property UseInsecure=true on the Servers > Web Servers > Web_server_name > Plug-in properties > Custom properties page in the IBM WebSphere Integrated Solution Console application for the failing WebSphere Profile. Then, restart your application server and web server for the changes to take affect.

This issue is documented in the following URL: http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg1PM85452 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg1PM85452]

- Open a session to the IBM WebSphere Integrated Solution Console for your WebSphere Profile.
- Expand Servers -> Server Types and click on "Web servers".

Screen shot of the WAS ISC showing Servers -> Server Types - Web servers [/support/docview.wss?uid=nas8N1019946&aid=1]

- Click on your HTTP Server instance name.
- Click on the "Plug-in properties" link under "Additional Properties" on the right-hand side of the screen.

Screen shot of the WAS ISC showing Plug-in properties under Additional Properties on the Web Servers page. [/support/docview.wss?uid=nas8N1019946&aid=2]
- Click on "Custom Properties" on the right-hand side of the screen.

Screen shot of the WAS ISC showing Custom Properties under Additional Properties on the Plug-in Properties page [/support/docview.wss?uid=nas8N1019946&aid=3]

question: How do I run the prerequisite scan for jazzSM? How do I run the prerequisite scan?
ground_truth:
Based on the information provided in the context, here are the steps to run the prerequisite scan for JazzSM:

1. Identify the product codes for both DASH (Dashboard Application Service Hub) and JazzSM.

2. Run the prerequisite scanner script, passing both the DASH and JazzSM product codes:

```
prereq_checker.bat <DASH_product_code> <JazzSM_product_code>
```

This will provide a detailed listing of the prerequisites for both DASH and JazzSM, showing which items have passed and which items have failed. 

The key points are:

- You need to pass the product codes for both DASH and JazzSM to the prerequisite scanner, not just one or the other.
- This will ensure that all the necessary prerequisites are checked and reported on before attempting the installation.
- Addressing any failed prerequisites is required before the installation will succeed.

So in summary, the steps are to identify the product codes and then run the prerequisite scanner script passing both of those codes to get the full set of prerequisite information.

question: Subnodes are still present on TEP after unconfiguring SOA DC After unconfiguring the ITCAM for SOA DC via the unconfig.sh script, I can still see SOA subnodes and workspaces on TEP.The Websphere instances were restarted and are no longer collecting data and forwarding to the D4 agent.I have shut down the ITCAM agent and deleted the offline entries for the managed systems, but the subnodes appear back once you restart the ITCAM agent.How can I remove them?
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, here are the steps to remove the SOA subnodes and workspaces from the TEP (Tivoli Enterprise Portal) after unconfiguring the ITCAM for SOA DC (Data Collector):

1. Stop the SOA Agent.

2. In the TEP, right-click on each of the SOA subnodes and select "Clear offline entry". This will remove the offline entries from the TEP.

3. Delete the information related to the removed SOA subnodes from the `KD4.dc.properties` file.

   - The entries to be removed will look similar to this:
     ```
     8.<DP hostname>.monitor=on
     8.<DP hostname>.log=error
     8.<DP hostname>.trace=off
     8.<DP hostname>.control.count=1
     8.<DP hostname>.control.1=*;*;*;*;*;none
     8.<DP hostname>.filter.control.count=0
     ```
   - Remove all the entries related to the deleted SOA subnodes and save the file.

4. Remove the following directory to erase the old records related to the SOA subnodes:
   - `<SOA_BASE_DIR>/KD4/logs/KD4.DCA.CACHE/persist`
   - `<SOA_BASE_DIR>` is the installation directory of the ITCAM for SOA agent (e.g., `<ITM_HOME>\TMAITM6` on Windows, `<ITM_HOME>/<platform>/d4` on Unix).

5. Restart the SOA Agent.

6. In the TEP, the deleted SOA subnodes and workspaces should no longer be visible.

If the subnodes and workspaces still appear after following these steps, you can try the following additional steps:

1. Use the `tacmd cleanMS` command to clear all offline managed systems entries. This command will remove all offline entries in a single step.
   - Before running `tacmd cleanMS`, you need to log in to the TEMS (Tivoli Enterprise Monitoring Server) using the `tacmd login` command.

2. If the issue persists, you may need to restart the Tivoli Enterprise Monitoring Server (TEMS) to ensure that all the changes are reflected in the TEP.

By following these steps, you should be able to remove the SOA subnodes and workspaces from the TEP after unconfiguring the ITCAM for SOA Data Collector.

     ```
     8.<DP hostname>.control.1=*;*;*;*;*;none
     8.<DP hostname>.filter.control.count=0
     ```
   - Remove all the entries related to the deleted SOA subnodes and save the file.

4. Remove the following directory to erase the old records related to the SOA subnodes:
   - `<SOA_BASE_DIR>/KD4/logs/KD4.DCA.CACHE/persist`
   - `<SOA_BASE_DIR>` is the installation directory of the ITCAM for SOA agent (e.g., `<ITM_HOME>\TMAITM6` on Windows, `<ITM_HOME>/<platform>/d4` on Unix).

5. Restart the SOA Agent.

6. In the TEP, the deleted SOA subnodes and workspaces should no longer be visible.

If the subnodes and workspaces still appear after following these steps, you can try the following additional steps:

1. Use the `tacmd cleanMS` command to clear all offline managed systems entries. This command will remove all offline entries in a single step.
   - Before running `tacmd cleanMS`, you need to log in to the TEMS (Tivoli Enterprise Monitoring Server) using the `tacmd login` command.

2. If the issue persists, you may need to restart the Tivoli Enterprise Monitoring Server (TEMS) to ensure that all the changes are reflected in the TEP.

By following these steps, you should be able to remove the SOA subnodes and workspaces from the TEP after unconfiguring the ITCAM for SOA Data Collector.
reference_context:
CAMSOAALL CAMSOATEPDATA same node leaf delete SOA TEPS workspace entry TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

THIS DOCUMENT APPLIES ONLY TO THE FOLLOWING LANGUAGE VERSION(S):
 US English 

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Need to remove the nodes with same name (there are several node names which is the same) and leave only 1 node name in TEPS tree view. 

SYMPTOM
Several different causes can result in the following issues. 

Issue A : Same node names exist and you want to remove all the same-named nodes and leave only 1 in tree. 

Issue B : Individual leaf (node) names for different devices. The individual leaf nodes are with the same name and only one of which reports activity. 


CAUSE
Issue B : Mostly it is due to the too long hostnames /long and similar starting server names.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
 

Issue A. (see symptom above for description) 

 Here is how to remove/trim the same node name down to 1 node (with the name) left in TEPS view. 


1. Stop the SOA Agent. 
2. Click (with 2nd mouse) the subnode that you want to remove, and choose "Clear offline entry" 
3. From KD4.dc.properties file, remove all the entry about the host/server that you deleted from TEPS and save it. 

 Example : If you deleted the entry related to Data Power, the related entry (to be removed) looks like this.
8.<DP hostname>.monitor=on
8.<DP hostname>.log=error
8.<DP hostname>.trace=off
8.<DP hostname>.control.count=1
8.<DP hostname>.control.1=*;*;*;*;*;none
8.<DP hostname>.filter.control.count=0 

 
4. Remove (windows: <ITM_HOME>\TMAITM6\KD4\logs\KD4.DCA.CACHE\persist, unix: <ITM_HOME>/<platform>/d4/KD4/logs/KD4.DCA.CACHE\persist) directory. 

 
5. Restart SOA Agent. 
6. From TEPS view, you should be able to see the deleted entry is gone. 

 

Issue B. (see symptom above for description) 

1. Change the file KD4.dc.properties (windows: <ITM_HOME>\TMAITM6\KD4\config, unix: <ITM_HOME>/<platform>/d4/KD4/config) 

Change kd4.ira.useCTIRAHostname=0 to 1. 

2. Change the Hostname to shorter name (ex: from "IBMHostname5" to "B5") - so that the remaining field of the whole node name will be filled with more characters of front portion of server name - by following description below - 

(in ManageTivoli Enterprise Monitoring Services (MTEMS) 

 a. Stop SOA Agent -> click right mouse on "SOA Agent". 

 b. Click "Advanced" -> "Edit Variables" 

 c. The screens will show as below (ITCAM for SOA: Override Local Variable Settings). 

 d. Choose "CTIRA_HOSTNAME" and click "Edit" as shown. 

 e. The "Edit Environment Setting Override" screen will pop up. Change the name to shorter hostname, preferrably that hostname length of 2) -> click OK. 


[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21645365&aid=1] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21645365&aid=1] 

3. Perform all the steps in Issue A. 


 

PRODUCT ALIAS/SYNONYM
 ITCAM4SOA ITCAM for SOA

question: I need to move my perpetual license of SPSS v24 to my new machine. I have my authorization code first given to me, but since we did not renew our support contract we can't seem to download the software. I was originally told that I could download the trial version and it would have the authorization wizard where I could input the code and move forward. But the only downloadable version is subscription version with no authorization wizard. How do I move forward?
ground_truth:
Based on the information provided in the TECHNOTE, it seems that you are facing a challenge in moving your perpetual license of SPSS v24 to a new machine since your Subscription and Maintenance has expired.

Here's what the TECHNOTE suggests you can do:

1. You cannot download the software from Passport Advantage Online if your Subscription and Maintenance has expired. The downloadable versions are only available for those with active Subscription and Maintenance.

2. However, you mention that you have the original authorization code. In this case, the TECHNOTE suggests that you can order the Media CD copy of the software your company had originally purchased. This Media CD will have the version of SPSS that was available up to the time your Subscription and Maintenance had expired.

3. You can then use the authorization code you have to license the software on your new machine using the License Authorization Wizard that comes with the software.

The key steps seem to be:

1. Contact your local IBM Sales Department to order the Media CD of the SPSS v24 software.
2. Use the original authorization code you have to license the software on your new machine using the License Authorization Wizard.

This should allow you to move your perpetual license of SPSS v24 to your new machine, even though your Subscription and Maintenance has expired. The TECHNOTE indicates this is the recommended approach in your situation.

question: How can I view the channels that are serached by auto program function and How can I view first five favorite channel?
ground_truth:
To view the channels that are searched by the auto program function, you can access the Channel List screen by pressing the right directional button on your remote control. From there, you can select "All Channels" to display the channels found during the Auto Program channel search.

To view the first five favorite channels, you can also access the Channel List screen by pressing the right directional button on your remote control. From there, you can select "Favorites" and then choose Favorites 1 from the five favorites lists displayed on the screen. This will show you the channels in the first favorites list.

question: How do I fix the missing/wrong color issue ?
ground_truth:
To fix the missing/wrong color issue on your TV, you can try the following steps:

1. Run the Start Picture Test to determine if the problem is caused by the TV's settings. 
2. Adjust the color settings by going to Picture Expert Settings and modifying the Color and Tint (G/R) settings.
3. Ensure that the video input connectors are connected properly to the external device.
4. If the colors are still incorrect, try adjusting the Picture Mode settings such as Brightness and Sharpness.
5. Check if Power Saving Mode is enabled and disable it.
6. If the issue persists, try resetting the picture settings in the Picture Expert Settings or change the Picture Mode to standard 16:9 settings.
7. If you are using AV or analog equipment, make sure the adapter is connected to the correct input port on the TV.
8. If none of the above steps resolve the issue, contact Samsung's Call Center for further assistance.
all_answers:
To fix the missing/wrong color issue on your TV, you can try the following steps:

2. Adjust the color settings by going to Picture Expert Settings and modifying the Color and Tint (G/R) settings.
3. Ensure that the video input connectors are connected properly to the external device.
4. If the colors are still incorrect, try adjusting the Picture Mode settings such as Brightness and Sharpness.
5. Check if Power Saving Mode is enabled and disable it.
6. If the issue persists, try resetting the picture settings in the Picture Expert Settings or change the Picture Mode to standard 16:9 settings.
7. If you are using AV or analog equipment, make sure the adapter is connected to the correct input port on the TV.
8. If none of the above steps resolve the issue, contact Samsung's Call Center for further assistance.

question: How can I connect my mobile device to the TV?
ground_truth:
To connect your mobile device to the TV, you can follow these steps:

1. Make sure your TV supports SmartThings and that you are signed in to your Samsung account on the TV.
2. Start the SmartThings app on your mobile device.
3. Tap on Add Device on the dashboard of the SmartThings app.
4. Connectable TVs will be searched for - select the model of your TV from the search results.
5. Enter the PIN number displayed on the TV screen.
6. Once the TV is registered with your Samsung account, you can use the SmartThings app to control the TV.
7. Make sure both your mobile device and the TV are connected to the same network for optimal functionality.
all_answers:
To connect your mobile device to the TV, you can follow these steps:

1. Make sure your TV supports SmartThings and that you are signed in to your Samsung account on the TV.
2. Start the SmartThings app on your mobile device.
3. Tap on Add Device on the dashboard of the SmartThings app.
4. Connectable TVs will be searched for - select the model of your TV from the search results.
5. Enter the PIN number displayed on the TV screen.
6. Once the TV is registered with your Samsung account, you can use the SmartThings app to control the TV.
7. Make sure both your mobile device and the TV are connected to the same network for optimal functionality.
reference_context: Connecting to the TV via the SmartThings app. Turn on the TV. Start the SmartThings app on your mobile device. Tap Add Device on the dashboard of the SmartThings app. Connectable TVs are searched for. From the search results, select the model of your TV. Enter the PIN number displayed on the TV screen. Once the TV is registered with your Samsung account, you can use the SmartThings app to control the TV. If your TV is not found on your mobile device, turn both of them off and on, and then try again. If you have more than one TV, you can easily select the TV you want by setting different TV names at Settings General System Manager Device Name

question: How do I fix odd sound of speaker?
ground_truth: To fix an odd sound coming from the speakers of the TV, you should first check the volume control of your TV and the external device (such as a cable box or DVD player) connected to your TV. If the issue persists, you can reboot the connected device by disconnecting and then reconnecting its power cable. Additionally, you should ensure that the audio cable is connected to the correct audio output connector on the external device. If there is still an issue, check the signal information for antenna or cable connections as a low signal level may cause sound distortions. If the problem continues, seek assistance from the manufacturer of the audio device connected to your TV.
all_answers: To fix an odd sound coming from the speakers of the TV, you should first check the volume control of your TV and the external device (such as a cable box or DVD player) connected to your TV. If the issue persists, you can reboot the connected device by disconnecting and then reconnecting its power cable. Additionally, you should ensure that the audio cable is connected to the correct audio output connector on the external device. If there is still an issue, check the signal information for antenna or cable connections as a low signal level may cause sound distortions. If the problem continues, seek assistance from the manufacturer of the audio device connected to your TV.

Check the volume control of your TV, and then check the volume control of the external device (cable box or satellite box, DVD, Blu-ray, etc.) connected to your TV. Set Sound Output TV Speaker Settings Sound Sound Output If you are using an external device, check the device's audio output option. For example, you may need to change your cable box's audio option to HDMI if the box connected to your TV is using an HDMI cable. To listen to computer sound, connect an external speaker to the computer's audio output connector. If your TV has a headphone jack, make sure there is nothing plugged into it. question: How can I search for the channels?
ground_truth: To search for channels, you can use the Channel List feature on your TV. Press the button on the remote to open Smart Hub, then use the directional buttons to move to Live TV and then to Channel List. This will display a list of channels with the program currently on. question: Explain the steps how to do Schedule Recording while watching a program?
ground_truth:
To do a Schedule Recording while watching a program, follow these steps:

1. Press the Select button while watching the program to display the program info window.
2. Use the left or right directional button to select a scheduled program that you want to record.
3. Press the Select button.
4. Select "Schedule Recording" from the options presented on the screen.
5. This will set up a scheduled recording of the selected program.
6. The program will be scheduled to record at the specified time, and you will be returned to watching the current program.

These steps allow you to easily schedule a recording of a program while already watching another program.

question: How can I use HDMI UHD Color?
ground_truth: To use HDMI UHD Color, you need to access the settings on your TV. Go to General > External Device Manager > HDMI UHD Color. If an external UHD player is connected to the TV, use the HDMI UHD Color function to convert the TV's HDMI connector to a mode optimized for the UHD 50P/60P signals. You can select the HDMI connector you want to use for HDMI UHD Color and then press the Select button to set the HDMI UHD Color function to On. This will enable support for up to UHD 50P/60P 4:4:4 and 4:2:2 input signals. If your external device only supports UHD 24 or 30 Hz frequency or any FHD frequency, the HDMI UHD Color function may not be available, and you should set HDMI UHD Color to Off in that case.
all_answers: To use HDMI UHD Color, you need to access the settings on your TV. Go to General > External Device Manager > HDMI UHD Color. If an external UHD player is connected to the TV, use the HDMI UHD Color function to convert the TV's HDMI connector to a mode optimized for the UHD 50P/60P signals. You can select the HDMI connector you want to use for HDMI UHD Color and then press the Select button to set the HDMI UHD Color function to On. This will enable support for up to UHD 50P/60P 4:4:4 and 4:2:2 input signals. If your external device only supports UHD 24 or 30 Hz frequency or any FHD frequency, the HDMI UHD Color function may not be available, and you should set HDMI UHD Color to Off in that case.

The HDMI connection with HDMI UHD Color set to Off supports up to UHD 50P/60P 4:2:0 input signals, while the HDMI connection with HDMI UHD Color set to supports up to UHD 50P/60P 4:4:4 and 4:2:2 input signals. question: Can you explain Ambient Mode?
ground_truth: Ambient Mode is a feature available on some TVs that allows users to set up and enjoy visual information and design when the TV is not in use. In Ambient Mode, the TV consumes less power and automatically adjusts brightness based on the surrounding light conditions using an illuminance sensor. There are different states of Ambient Mode, such as getting dark state and black screen state, where the TV screen changes automatically according to the surroundings, becoming darker when it gets dark and turning off when it reaches a specific level of darkness. Users can also change settings like screen brightness, color tone, and auto brightness for Ambient Mode. Additionally, users can select different categories of content, such as Decor, Info, and Photo, to display on the Ambient Mode screen. The TV can be switched between Ambient Mode and regular TV mode by pressing buttons on the remote control.

question: how do I fix low volume issue?
ground_truth:
To fix the low volume issue on your TV, you can try the following steps:

1. Check the volume control of your TV and the external device (cable box, satellite box, DVD player, Blu-ray player, etc.) connected to your TV.
2. If you are using an external device, make sure to check the device's audio output option. For example, you may need to change the audio output option to HDMI if the box connected to your TV is using an HDMI cable.
3. If you are listening to computer sound, connect an external speaker to the computer's audio output connector.
4. Make sure there is nothing plugged into the headphone jack of your TV.
5. Reboot the connected device by disconnecting and then reconnecting the device's power cable.
6. Check if the Digital Output Audio Format is set to Dolby Digital+. If you are using a receiver that does not support Dolby Digital Plus, you will hear no sound when you select Dolby Digital+.
7. Make sure the audio cable is connected to the correct audio output connector on the external device.
8. For antenna or cable connections, check the signal information, as a low signal level may cause sound distortions.

Additionally, for better audio quality, consider using an AV receiver when connecting external audio devices. If you connect an external audio device using an optical cable, the Sound Output setting is automatically changed to the connected device. To manually change the Sound Output setting, you can use the Quick Settings screen or the Settings screen on your TV.

3. If you are listening to computer sound, connect an external speaker to the computer's audio output connector.

question: How do I check scheduled viewings?
ground_truth:
To check scheduled viewings, you can follow these steps:

1. Open the Smart Hub by pressing the button on your remote.
2. Use the left or right directional button to move to Live TV.
3. Press the up directional button to move to the top row.
4. Use the left or right directional button to move to Schedule Manager Recordings.
5. Press Select to open Schedule Manager Recordings.
6. Use the up directional button to move to the Schedules menu.
7. Use the down directional button to move to the list of programs scheduled to view.

From there, you can see the list of programs that are scheduled for viewing.

question: How to update TV's software through USB device?
ground_truth:
To update the TV's software through a USB device, follow these steps:

1. Download the update file from the Samsung website to your computer.
2. Save the update package in the USB device's top-level folder.
3. Connect the USB device to the TV.
4. The TV will automatically detect and install the update from the USB device. 

It is important to remember not to turn off the TV's power until the update is complete, as the TV will turn off and on automatically during the update process. Additionally, after completing the software update, the video and audio settings will be reset to their default values.

question: Please instruct how to record any program?
ground_truth:
To record any program, you can use the following steps: 

1. If the program is currently on air, open the program guide and move to the program you want to record. Then, press and hold the Select button. A pop-up menu will appear. 
2. Use the up and down directional buttons to navigate to the "Record" option in the pop-up menu, then press Select to start recording the program immediately. 
3. The recording will automatically end when the program ends. 
4. To stop recording the program, press and hold the Select button again, and in the menu that appears, select "Stop Recording." 
5. If you want to schedule a recording for a program that will air later, move to the program in the Guide, press Select to access the pop-up menu, then navigate to the "Schedule Recording" option.
6. A message will confirm that the program has been set to record, and an icon will be displayed next to the program title.
7. To check if a program is set to record or to cancel a scheduled recording, press Select on the program and follow the prompts accordingly. 

These steps will guide you through recording any program using the instant and schedule recording options available.
all_answers:
To record any program, you can use the following steps: 

1. If the program is currently on air, open the program guide and move to the program you want to record. Then, press and hold the Select button. A pop-up menu will appear. 
2. Use the up and down directional buttons to navigate to the "Record" option in the pop-up menu, then press Select to start recording the program immediately. 
3. The recording will automatically end when the program ends. 
4. To stop recording the program, press and hold the Select button again, and in the menu that appears, select "Stop Recording." 
5. If you want to schedule a recording for a program that will air later, move to the program in the Guide, press Select to access the pop-up menu, then navigate to the "Schedule Recording" option.
6. A message will confirm that the program has been set to record, and an icon will be displayed next to the program title.
7. To check if a program is set to record or to cancel a scheduled recording, press Select on the program and follow the prompts accordingly. 

These steps will guide you through recording any program using the instant and schedule recording options available.

Recording automatically ends at the time when the program ends. To open the Guide and stop recording the program, press and hold the Select button. The menu is displayed and there is now a Stop Recording option instead of a Record option. Move to the Stop Recording option, and then press the Select button. To record a program that is on later, move to the program in the Guide , and then press Select. You will get a pop-up menu. Use the up and down directional button to move within this menu. question: How to create new account in SmartThings ?
ground_truth:
To create a new account in SmartThings, you need to follow these steps:

1. Turn on the TV.
2. Start the SmartThings app on your mobile device.
3. Tap "Add Device" on the dashboard of the SmartThings app.
4. Connectable TVs are searched for.
5. From the search results, select the model of your TV.
6. Enter the PIN number displayed on the TV screen.
7. Once the TV is registered with your Samsung account, you can use the SmartThings app to control the TV.

question: I want to  enter into Ambient mode. How can I do that?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, here are the steps to enter Ambient Mode:

1. Press the button on your Samsung TV remote control.
2. This will bring up the Ambient Mode browser screen, where you can select content and change Ambient Mode settings.

The key points are:

- Pressing the button on the remote control will enter Ambient Mode.
- If the TV is turned off, pressing the button will turn the TV on directly in Ambient Mode.
- Using a remote control other than the Samsung TV remote may have restrictions on accessing Ambient Mode, as it is a QLED TV-specific feature.

So in summary, the main way to enter Ambient Mode is by pressing the dedicated button on your Samsung TV remote control.

question: How can I view the channels that are serached by auto program function and How can I view first five favorite channel?
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, here are the steps to view the channels searched by the Auto Program function and to view the first five favorite channels:

1. To view the channels searched by the Auto Program function:
   - Go to the Live TV Channel List
   - Press the right directional button on your remote control
   - Select "All Channels" to display the channels that the TV found during the Auto Program channel search.

2. To view the first five favorite channels:
   - Go to the Live TV Channel List
   - Press the right directional button on your remote control
   - Select "Favorites"
   - Select the desired favorites list (Favorites 1 to Favorites 5) on the screen
   - The channels in the selected favorites list will be displayed.

The context mentions that only favorites lists that contain registered channels will display channels. So, if you have set up your favorite channel lists, you can switch between them and view the first five favorite channels.

question: How do I fix the missing/wrong color issue ?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, here are the steps to fix the missing or wrong color issue on your TV:

1. Run the Start Picture Test to determine if the problem is caused by the TV or an external device.

2. If the test indicates the problem is not caused by the TV, check the following:
   - Confirm the video input connectors are connected to the correct external device video output connectors.
   - If using a component cable, make sure the Pb, Pr, and Y jacks are plugged into their proper connectors.

3. If the colors are just a little too dark or bright, try adjusting the following settings:
   - Picture Expert Settings: Backlight, Contrast, Brightness, Sharpness, Color, Tint (G/R)

4. If the colors are completely wrong or missing, check if the Power Saving Mode has been enabled. Disable it if so.

5. Try resetting the picture settings by going to Picture > Expert Settings > Reset Picture.

6. If you are using AV or analog equipment, try disconnecting the adapter from the Component (Blue) input port and connecting it to the AV (Yellow) input port.

7. Check if Grayscale is set to on under Settings > General > Accessibility > Grayscale and turn it off if so.

By following these troubleshooting steps, you should be able to resolve the missing or wrong color issue on your TV.
all_answers:
Based on the context provided, here are the steps to fix the missing or wrong color issue on your TV:

1. Run the Start Picture Test to determine if the problem is caused by the TV or an external device.

2. If the test indicates the problem is not caused by the TV, check the following:
   - Confirm the video input connectors are connected to the correct external device video output connectors.
   - If using a component cable, make sure the Pb, Pr, and Y jacks are plugged into their proper connectors.

3. If the colors are just a little too dark or bright, try adjusting the following settings:
   - Picture Expert Settings: Backlight, Contrast, Brightness, Sharpness, Color, Tint (G/R)

4. If the colors are completely wrong or missing, check if the Power Saving Mode has been enabled. Disable it if so.

5. Try resetting the picture settings by going to Picture > Expert Settings > Reset Picture.

6. If you are using AV or analog equipment, try disconnecting the adapter from the Component (Blue) input port and connecting it to the AV (Yellow) input port.

7. Check if Grayscale is set to on under Settings > General > Accessibility > Grayscale and turn it off if so.

By following these troubleshooting steps, you should be able to resolve the missing or wrong color issue on your TV.

question: How do I fix blurring issues on TV screen?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, here are the steps to fix blurring issues on the TV screen:

1. Run the Picture Test to determine if the issue is caused by the TV or an external device:
   - Go to Settings > Support > Self Diagnosis > Start Picture Test

2. If the Picture Test indicates an issue with the TV, try the following:
   - Adjust the TV's picture settings such as Backlight, Contrast, Brightness, Sharpness, and Color.
   - Go to Settings > Picture > Expert Settings and adjust these settings.

3. If the issue is not caused by the TV, check the following:
   - Ensure the video input connectors are properly connected to the correct external device video output connectors.
   - If using a component cable connection, confirm the Pb, Pr, and Y jacks are plugged into their proper connectors.

4. To address ghosting or blurring, try adjusting the Auto Motion Plus settings:
   - Go to Settings > Picture > Expert Settings > Auto Motion Plus

5. If the issue persists, you may need to contact Samsung's customer support for further assistance.

The key steps are to first determine the source of the issue, then adjust the TV's picture settings, check the connections, and finally use the Auto Motion Plus settings to address any blurring or ghosting.

question: What is the use of universal guide?
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, the Universal Guide app has the following uses:

1. Allows you to search for and enjoy various content such as TV shows, dramas, movies, sports broadcasts, and music in one place.

2. Can recommend content tailored to your preferences and notify you of new drama series.

3. Can be used on your mobile device with the Samsung SmartThings app to access and enjoy the content on your TV, as long as the necessary apps are installed on the TV.

5. Allows you to connect and control external devices using the Samsung Smart Remote through the Universal Remote feature (though this may not be supported on all models or geographical areas).

In summary, the main use of the Universal Guide app is to provide a centralized platform to search, discover, and enjoy various types of content across different devices and services.

ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, the key features of the Bixby guide are:

1. It is accessed by pressing the Bixby button on the Samsung Smart Remote once.

2. When you press the Bixby button for the first time, the "Using Bixby" button appears at the bottom of the screen. Pressing the Select button on the remote opens a popup window that displays a tutorial on how to use Bixby.

3. After the first time, pressing the Bixby button on the remote will make the "Enter My Bixby" button appear at the bottom of the screen. Pressing the Select button on the remote will take you to the "My Bixby" screen.

question: How do I record using time Timeshift function?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, here are the steps to record using the Timeshift function:

1. Connect a USB device to your device for recording. The Timeshift function requires a USB device for recording live TV.

2. To activate the Timeshift function while watching TV, press the button and then select "Timeshift", or press the button.

3. Once the Timeshift function is running, you can pause, rewind, or play back the live TV you are watching.

4. To record the program you are watching using Timeshift, press the button while the Timeshift function is running. This will display a menu with the following options:

   - Record: This option will immediately start recording the program you are watching.
   - Timeshift: This option will continue the Timeshift function.
   - Stop: This option will stop the recording or Timeshift function that is running.

So in summary, to record using the Timeshift function, you need to first activate Timeshift, then press the button and select the "Record" option from the menu that appears.

question: My IP auto setting failed. How to configure it?
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, here are the steps to configure the IP settings when the IP auto setting fails:

1. Make sure that the DHCP server is enabled on the router. If not, enable it.
2. Unplug the router and plug it back in to reset the connection.
3. If the issue persists, you may need to reset the router. Check the router's documentation for the reset process.
4. For a wireless connection, make sure you are entering the correct password for the wireless network.
5. If the issue still persists after these steps, you may need to manually configure the IP settings. Go to Settings > General > Network > Network Status > IP Settings and configure the IP address, subnet mask, gateway, and DNS values manually.

The key steps are ensuring the DHCP server is enabled, resetting the router, and then manually configuring the IP settings if the automatic IP assignment still fails.
all_answers:
Based on the provided context, here are the steps to configure the IP settings when the IP auto setting fails:

2. Unplug the router and plug it back in to reset the connection.
3. If the issue persists, you may need to reset the router. Check the router's documentation for the reset process.
4. For a wireless connection, make sure you are entering the correct password for the wireless network.
5. If the issue still persists after these steps, you may need to manually configure the IP settings. Go to Settings > General > Network > Network Status > IP Settings and configure the IP address, subnet mask, gateway, and DNS values manually.

The key steps are ensuring the DHCP server is enabled, resetting the router, and then manually configuring the IP settings if the automatic IP assignment still fails.

question: How can I connect my mobile device to the TV?
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, there are a few ways to connect your mobile device to the TV:

1. Using the SmartThings app:
   - Download and install the SmartThings app on your mobile device.
   - Connect your mobile device and TV to the same network.
   - Open the SmartThings app, tap "Add Device", and select your TV model from the search results.
   - Enter the PIN displayed on the TV screen to register your TV with your Samsung account.
   - Once connected, you can use the SmartThings app to control the TV.

2. Using Screen Mirroring or Smart View:
   - Ensure your mobile device supports a mirroring function like Screen Mirroring or Smart View.
   - Connect your mobile device and TV to the same network.
   - Use the mirroring function on your mobile device to connect and mirror the screen to the TV.

3. Using Wi-Fi Direct:
   - Ensure your mobile device supports the Wi-Fi Direct function.
   - Connect your mobile device and TV to each other using Wi-Fi Direct.

The key is to make sure your mobile device and TV are connected to the same network or can connect directly via a supported function, such as Screen Mirroring, Smart View, or Wi-Fi Direct.

question: How do I set scheduled viewing time?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, there are a few ways to set up scheduled viewing on your TV:

1. Using the Guide Screen:
   - On the Guide screen, select the program you want to schedule.
   - Press and hold the Select button, then choose "Schedule Viewing" from the pop-up menu.

2. Using the Program Info Screen:
   - While watching TV, press the Select button to bring up the Program Info window.
   - Select the program you want to schedule using the left/right directional buttons.
   - Press the Select button, then choose "Schedule Viewing" to schedule that program.

3. Using the Schedule Viewing options:
   - Go to the "Live TV Schedule Manager" (in the U.S. and Canada) or "Recordings Schedules" (in other countries).
   - Select "Add Viewing" and set the Antenna Channel, Repeat, Date, and Start Time for the program you want to schedule.
   - Once you've set the details, select "Save" to finalize the scheduled viewing.

Before setting up the scheduled viewing, make sure to first set the correct time on your TV's clock, which can be found in the "Settings > General > System Manager > Time & Clock" menu.

question: What is source and how to serch data for channels?

   - When you select a connected external device (e.g., Blu-ray player, game console) on the Source screen, the output of the selected device is displayed on the TV's screen.

   - When a USB device is connected, a pop-up message appears that lets you switch easily to the media content listed on the device.

In summary, the "Source" refers to the input signal or connected external device, and the "Channel List" allows you to search and navigate through the available digital channels, including favorite and locked channels, as well as the option to select the DTV mode (Air or Cable).

question: How do I fix odd sound of speaker?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, here are the steps to fix an odd sound coming from the speakers:

1. Check the volume control on the TV and any connected external devices (cable box, DVD player, etc.). Make sure the volume is not set too high, which can cause distortion.

2. Try rebooting the connected external device by disconnecting and then reconnecting the power cable.

3. Check if the Digital Output Audio Format is set to Dolby Digital+. If the connected receiver doesn't support Dolby Digital+, it may cause audio issues.

4. Ensure the audio cable is connected to the correct audio output connector on the external device.

5. Check the signal information for the antenna or cable connections. A low signal level can cause sound distortions.

6. If using an external audio device connected via an optical cable, make sure to turn on the external device before connecting the optical cable. This will ensure the Sound Output setting is automatically changed to the connected device.

7. If the issue persists, the odd sound may be due to a problem with the connected audio device itself. In that case, you should contact the manufacturer of the audio device for further assistance.
all_answers:

3. Check if the Digital Output Audio Format is set to Dolby Digital+. If the connected receiver doesn't support Dolby Digital+, it may cause audio issues.

4. Ensure the audio cable is connected to the correct audio output connector on the external device.

5. Check the signal information for the antenna or cable connections. A low signal level can cause sound distortions.

6. If using an external audio device connected via an optical cable, make sure to turn on the external device before connecting the optical cable. This will ensure the Sound Output setting is automatically changed to the connected device.

7. If the issue persists, the odd sound may be due to a problem with the connected audio device itself. In that case, you should contact the manufacturer of the audio device for further assistance.

question: How can I use HDMI UHD Color?
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, here are the key points on how to use the HDMI UHD Color function:

1. Connect an external UHD player to the TV's HDMI connector.

2. Go to the TV's settings menu and navigate to the "General" > "External Device Manager" > "HDMI UHD Color" option.

3. Select the HDMI connector you want to use for HDMI UHD Color and press the Select button to set it to "On".

4. When HDMI UHD Color is set to "On", the TV supports up to UHD 50P/60P input signals with 4:4:4 and 4:2:2 chroma sampling.

5. If the external device only supports UHD 24 or 30 Hz frequency or any FHD frequency, the HDMI UHD Color function may not be available. In this case, you should set HDMI UHD Color to "Off".

6. When HDMI UHD Color is set to "Off", the TV supports up to UHD 50P/60P input signals with 4:2:0 chroma sampling.

So, in summary, enable the HDMI UHD Color function when connecting an external UHD player to get the best possible video quality, but make sure the connected device supports the required UHD resolutions and frame rates.

4. When HDMI UHD Color is set to "On", the TV supports up to UHD 50P/60P input signals with 4:4:4 and 4:2:2 chroma sampling.

5. If the external device only supports UHD 24 or 30 Hz frequency or any FHD frequency, the HDMI UHD Color function may not be available. In this case, you should set HDMI UHD Color to "Off".

6. When HDMI UHD Color is set to "Off", the TV supports up to UHD 50P/60P input signals with 4:2:0 chroma sampling.

So, in summary, enable the HDMI UHD Color function when connecting an external UHD player to get the best possible video quality, but make sure the connected device supports the required UHD resolutions and frame rates.

question: Can you explain Ambient Mode?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, Ambient Mode on the TV is a feature that allows you to display visual information and designs on the TV screen even when the TV is not in the main viewing mode. Here are the key points about Ambient Mode:

1. Ambient Mode is a low-power state where the TV screen brightness is automatically adjusted based on the surrounding light level, using an illuminance sensor.

2. There are different screen states in Ambient Mode:
   - Ambient Mode: The TV screen displays information and designs in a low-power state.
   - Getting dark state: The TV screen becomes darker as the surroundings get darker.
   - Black screen state: If the surroundings get very dark or there is no user interaction, the TV goes into a soft-off state with a black screen.

3. In Ambient Mode, the TV can activate voice guides and process voice commands, but in the black screen state, it cannot.

4. You can access the Ambient Mode browser to select content like decorative screens, information widgets, and photos to display in Ambient Mode.

5. You can also adjust the brightness, color tone, auto-brightness, and set an auto-off timer for Ambient Mode.

6. The background color and pattern of Ambient Mode can be customized, including using a photo of a wall taken with the SmartThings app.

In summary, Ambient Mode allows the TV to display useful and visually appealing content in a low-power state, adjusting to the surrounding light conditions.

question: Can I configure Brightness?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, you can configure the brightness of the TV in several ways:

1. Ambient Light Detection: This feature automatically adjusts the brightness level of the TV according to the ambient light level to reduce power consumption. If the brightness level is too bright or too dark, you can manually adjust the Minimum Backlight to set the minimum screen brightness.

2. Backlight: You can manually adjust the Backlight setting under the Picture Expert Settings menu to configure the brightness of the screen.

3. Brightness: You can directly adjust the Brightness setting under the Picture Expert Settings menu to configure the screen brightness.

4. Contrast Enhancer: This feature can be used to automatically provide an optimal HDR (High Dynamic Range) effect, which can impact the overall brightness of the image.

5. Auto Brightness: In Ambient Mode, the TV can automatically adjust the screen brightness based on the surrounding light conditions.

So in summary, the context indicates that you can configure the brightness of the TV through various settings like Ambient Light Detection, Backlight, Brightness, Contrast Enhancer, and Auto Brightness in Ambient Mode.

question: Can I fix powering on issue?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, here are the steps you can try to fix powering on issues with your TV:

1. Confirm that the TV's power cord is connected correctly at both ends.

2. Make sure the remote control is operating normally.

3. Check that the antenna cable or cable TV cable is firmly connected.

4. If you have a cable box or satellite box, confirm that it is plugged in and turned on.

5. If your TV is not connected to a cable box or satellite box, run the Auto Program function to store the available channels.

6. If you have an analog cable box or satellite box, consider upgrading to a digital one to get better picture quality.

7. Use HDMI or Component cables to connect your external devices for HD picture quality.

8. Adjust the cable box or satellite box's video output resolution to 1080i or 720p.

9. If the issue persists, contact Samsung's Call Center for further assistance.

The context provides troubleshooting steps to address various TV issues, including powering on problems. By following these steps, you should be able to resolve the powering on issue with your TV.

question: how do I fix low volume issue?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, here are the steps to fix a low volume issue:

1. Check the volume control on your TV and make sure it is set to the maximum level.

2. Check the volume control on the external device (e.g. cable box, satellite box, DVD/Blu-ray player) connected to your TV and ensure it is also set to a suitable volume level.

3. If you are using an external device, check the audio output option on that device and make sure it is set correctly. For example, you may need to change the cable box's audio option to HDMI if it is connected to the TV via HDMI.

4. If your TV has a headphone jack, make sure there is nothing plugged into it, as this can affect the main speaker volume.

5. Reboot the connected external device by unplugging and then reconnecting the power cable.

6. Check if the Digital Output Audio Format is set to Dolby Digital+. If the receiver/audio device does not support Dolby Digital+, you may need to change this setting.

7. Ensure the audio cable is connected to the correct audio output connector on the external device.

8. If it's an antenna or cable connection, check the signal information as a low signal level may cause sound distortions.

Following these troubleshooting steps should help resolve the low volume issue on your TV.

7. Ensure the audio cable is connected to the correct audio output connector on the external device.

8. If it's an antenna or cable connection, check the signal information as a low signal level may cause sound distortions.

Following these troubleshooting steps should help resolve the low volume issue on your TV.

question: My software update over the Internet has failed. How do I fix this?
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, if your software update over the Internet has failed, you can try the following steps to fix it:

1. Update through a USB device:
   - Download the update package from the Samsung website to your computer.
   - Save the update package in the top-level folder of a USB flash drive.
   - Connect the USB device to your TV.
   - Go to Settings > Support > Software Update > Update Now and select the USB device to update the TV's software.

2. Check your internet connection:
   - Ensure that your TV has an active internet connection. The context mentions that "Updating from the Internet requires an active Internet connection."
   - If you're experiencing issues with your internet connection, try troubleshooting it or contacting your internet service provider for assistance.

3. Try the update again:
   - Go to Settings > Support > Software Update > Update Now or Try Now to attempt the software update over the internet again.
   - The context states that the "TV will turn off and on automatically after completing the software update," so be patient and do not turn off the TV during the update process.

4. Consider automatic updates:
   - If your TV is connected to the internet, you can enable the "Auto Update" feature, which will automatically update the TV's software in the background while you're watching.
   - This feature is mentioned in the context and can be enabled or disabled in the Settings > Support > Software Update menu.

By following these steps, you should be able to successfully update your TV's software either through a USB device or by troubleshooting and retrying the internet-based update.

question: How to turn TV in Ambient Mode?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, here are the steps to turn the TV into Ambient Mode:

1. Press the button on the remote control when the TV is turned off. This will turn on the TV in Ambient Mode.

2. To enter Ambient Mode when the TV is already turned on, press the button on the remote control.

The key points are:

- Pressing the button on the remote control will turn on the TV in Ambient Mode, whether the TV was previously turned off or on.
- Ambient Mode is a QLED TV-specific feature, so it may not be supported on all TV models.
- If you use a remote control other than the Samsung TV remote control, there may be restrictions to entering Ambient Mode.

question: TV audio is not being played through the receiver. What should I do?
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, if the TV audio is not being played through the receiver, you should try the following:

1. Check the Digital Output Audio Format setting on the TV. If it is set to Dolby Digital+, and the receiver does not support Dolby Digital+, you will not hear any sound. Try changing the Digital Output Audio Format to a different option that is supported by your receiver.

2. Ensure that the audio cable is connected to the correct audio output connector on the external device (e.g., cable box, satellite box, DVD player, etc.) that is connected to the TV.

3. Check the audio output setting on the connected external device. For example, if the device is connected to the TV via HDMI, you may need to change the audio output option to HDMI.

4. Reboot the connected external device by disconnecting and then reconnecting the power cable.

5. If you are using an HDMI-to-DVI cable to connect the external device to the TV, a separate audio cable is required, as the HDMI-to-DVI connection does not transmit audio. In this case, connect external speakers to the audio output connection of the computer.

6. Check the volume control on both the TV and the receiver to ensure they are set to an appropriate level.

By following these troubleshooting steps, you should be able to resolve the issue of the TV audio not being played through the receiver.
all_answers:

2. Ensure that the audio cable is connected to the correct audio output connector on the external device (e.g., cable box, satellite box, DVD player, etc.) that is connected to the TV.

3. Check the audio output setting on the connected external device. For example, if the device is connected to the TV via HDMI, you may need to change the audio output option to HDMI.

4. Reboot the connected external device by disconnecting and then reconnecting the power cable.

5. If you are using an HDMI-to-DVI cable to connect the external device to the TV, a separate audio cable is required, as the HDMI-to-DVI connection does not transmit audio. In this case, connect external speakers to the audio output connection of the computer.

6. Check the volume control on both the TV and the receiver to ensure they are set to an appropriate level.

By following these troubleshooting steps, you should be able to resolve the issue of the TV audio not being played through the receiver.

question: How do I change the current time on TV?
ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, there are a few ways to change the current time on the TV:

1. Setting the Clock Manually:
   - Go to Settings > General > System Manager > Time > Clock
   - Set the Clock Mode to "Manual"
   - Directly enter the current date and time using the directional buttons on the remote control.

2. Setting the Clock Automatically:
   - Go to Settings > General > System Manager > Time > Clock
   - Set the Clock Mode to "Auto"
   - This will automatically set the clock based on the TV's internet connection or digital broadcast signals.
   - If the TV is connected to a cable/satellite box, you'll need to set the time manually.

3. Adjusting the Clock for Daylight Saving Time (DST) and Time Zone:
   - Go to Settings > General > System Manager > Time > Clock
   - Adjust the Time Zone and DST settings to match your local time.
   - This is only available when the Clock Mode is set to "Auto".

4. Adjusting the Time Offset:
   - Go to Settings > General > System Manager > Time > Clock
   - If the TV fails to receive accurate time information, you can use the Time Offset setting to adjust the time.
   - This is only available when the Clock Mode is set to "Auto" and the TV is connected to the internet.

So in summary, you can change the current time on your TV either by manually setting the clock or by configuring the automatic clock settings based on your location and internet/broadcast connection.

question: How do I turn on High Contrast using Bixby?
ground_truth:
To turn on High Contrast using Bixby, follow these steps:

1. Press and hold the button on the Samsung Smart Remote.
2. While holding the button, say "Turn High Contrast on" and then release the button.
3. The TV will confirm the command and turn on High Contrast mode, which changes the screen to white text on a black background for better visibility.

The context provided states that you can use voice commands with Bixby to control accessibility features like High Contrast. question: What do I do if wireless network connection failed?

2. Select the correct wireless router from the available options.

5. Enter the correct password for the wireless router, if required.

question: Can I fix low volume issue?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, here are the steps you can try to fix a low volume issue on your TV:

1. Check the volume control on your TV and any external devices (cable/satellite box, DVD/Blu-ray player, etc.) connected to the TV. Ensure the volume is turned up on both the TV and the external device.

2. If using an external device, check the audio output settings on that device and ensure it is set to output audio through HDMI or the correct connector.

3. If your TV has a headphone jack, make sure nothing is plugged into it, as this can mute the TV's speakers.

4. Reboot the connected external device by disconnecting and then reconnecting the power cable.

5. Check the Digital Output Audio Format setting on your TV and ensure it is not set to Dolby Digital+ if your receiver does not support that format (you will get no sound in that case).

6. Adjust the Balance, Equalizer, and Audio Delay settings in the TV's sound expert settings to see if you can improve the volume and sound quality.

7. Try running the Start Sound Test in the TV's self-diagnosis menu to see if the issue is with the TV's internal speakers or an external connection.

By following these troubleshooting steps, you should be able to identify and resolve the low volume issue on your TV.

2. If using an external device, check the audio output settings on that device and ensure it is set to output audio through HDMI or the correct connector.

3. If your TV has a headphone jack, make sure nothing is plugged into it, as this can mute the TV's speakers.

4. Reboot the connected external device by disconnecting and then reconnecting the power cable.

5. Check the Digital Output Audio Format setting on your TV and ensure it is not set to Dolby Digital+ if your receiver does not support that format (you will get no sound in that case).

6. Adjust the Balance, Equalizer, and Audio Delay settings in the TV's sound expert settings to see if you can improve the volume and sound quality.

7. Try running the Start Sound Test in the TV's self-diagnosis menu to see if the issue is with the TV's internal speakers or an external connection.

question: How can I turn on ambient mode on TV screen. Can you explain about that?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, here's how you can turn on the Ambient Mode on your TV screen:

1. To enter Ambient Mode when the TV is turned off, press the dedicated button on the remote control.

2. When the TV is turned off, pressing the button will turn on the TV directly in Ambient Mode.

3. Ambient Mode allows you to view beautiful screens, various visual information, and notifications on your TV screen.

4. In Ambient Mode, the TV screen consumes less power and the brightness is automatically adjusted by the illuminance sensor based on the surrounding brightness.

5. Ambient Mode has different screen states - the normal Ambient Mode, the "Getting dark" state where the screen becomes darker as the surroundings get darker, and the "Black screen" state where the TV turns soft-off if the surroundings get too dark or there's no external operation.

6. You can change the auto brightness settings for the Ambient Mode in the TV settings.

7. Note that Ambient Mode is a feature specific to QLED TVs, so it may not be supported on all TV models.

So in summary, to turn on Ambient Mode, simply press the dedicated button on your TV remote control when the TV is turned off. This will activate the Ambient Mode features on your TV screen.

question: How can I select channel filter option ?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, you can select the channel filter option by following these steps:

1. Open the Channel List by pressing the button on your remote to open the Smart Hub, then pressing the left or right directional buttons to move to Live TV, and the up directional button to move to the top row, followed by the right directional button to move to Channel List and pressing the Select button.

2. Once in the Channel List, press the right directional button to move to the Category List.

3. In the Category List, use the up and down directional buttons to move between the options, which include "All Channels" and "Favorites".

4. To select a category, press the Select button. The channel list will now show only the channels in the selected category (either "All Channels" or the selected "Favorites" list).

So, in summary, you can select the channel filter option by navigating to the Category List within the Channel List and choosing either "All Channels" or the desired "Favorites" list.

question: How to update TV's software through USB device?
ground_truth:
Based on the context provided, here are the steps to update the TV's software through a USB device:

1. Download the update package from the Samsung website to your computer.
2. Save the update package in the top-level folder of a USB flash drive.
3. Connect the USB flash drive to the TV.
4. Go to the Settings > Support > Software Update menu on the TV.
5. Select the "Update Now" or "Update through USB" option.
6. The TV will detect the update package on the USB drive and start the software update process.
7. DO NOT turn off the TV's power until the update is complete. The TV will turn off and on automatically once the update is finished.
8. After the update, the video and audio settings will be reset to their default values.

Remember, the key steps are to download the update package from the Samsung website, save it in the top-level folder of a USB drive, and then connect the USB drive to the TV to initiate the software update process.

question: How do I enable/disable light effect?
ground_truth:
Based on the given context, there are a few ways to enable or disable light effects on the TV:

1. Ambient Light Detection:
   - This feature automatically adjusts the brightness level of the TV according to the ambient light level to reduce power consumption.
   - If the screen brightness is too bright or dark, you can select "Minimum Backlight" to manually adjust the minimum screen brightness.

2. Motion Lighting:
   - This feature adjusts the brightness in response to on-screen movements to reduce power consumption.

3. Ambient Mode Settings:
   - In the Ambient Mode browser screen, you can adjust the "Brightness" and "Color Tone" settings to change the appearance of the Ambient Mode screen.
   - You can also turn off the "Auto Brightness" setting to disable the automatic brightness adjustment based on ambient light.

So in summary, you can enable or disable light effects by configuring the Ambient Light Detection, Motion Lighting, and Ambient Mode settings on your TV. Adjusting the brightness, minimum backlight, and auto brightness settings will allow you to control the light effects.

question: What is the structure of Hantaan virus?
ground_truth: The structure of Hantaan virus is spherical or ovoid, composed of 20-30% fat, >50% protein, 7% carbohydrates, and 2% RNA. The genome comprises three negative sense, single-stranded RNAs that consist of the small, medium, and large segments encoding the nucleoprotein, envelope glycoproteins, and viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase, respectively. The outer membrane of Hantaan virus is composed of Gn and Gc glycoproteins.

The outer membrane of Hantaan virus is composed of Gn and Gc glycoproteins.

question: What is  an example of anti-viral defense in bats?

For example, some bats have an antiviral immune response called the interferon pathway perpetually switched on. In most other mammals, having such a hyper-vigilant immune response would cause harmful inflammation. Bats, however, have adapted anti-inflammatory traits that protect them from such harm, include the loss of certain genes that normally promote inflammation. question: What has been the application of phage display technology?
ground_truth: The application of phage display technology has been the production of protein libraries, specific binder screening with biopanning, and the modification of the filamentous phage genome to display polypeptides as fusions to coat proteins on the virion surface.
all_answers: The application of phage display technology has been the production of protein libraries, specific binder screening with biopanning, and the modification of the filamentous phage genome to display polypeptides as fusions to coat proteins on the virion surface.
reference_context: Phage display technology has facilitated the production of protein libraries, which are formed with large numbers of phage particles displaying different molecules . Specific binder screening with biopanning allows the enrichment of the desired molecule . The first step is the incubation of the display library with an immobilized surface of the entire cell. The non-binding phages are then removed by extensive washing and the binders are eluted by acid or salt buffer. Then, binders are amplified using an appropriate bacterial host cell such as E. coli. To obtain highaffinity targets, up to five rounds of biopanning are performed .

question: What is the treatment for MERS-COV?
ground_truth: The treatment for MERS-CoV is supportive and symptomatic, as there is currently no specific treatment or vaccine available. Supportive care, such as respiratory support, hydration, antipyretics, and infection control, is the mainstay of treatment for MERS-CoV. Additionally, preventative actions such as hand hygiene and covering the nose and mouth during sneezing and coughing are recommended. Various therapeutic options have been investigated, but no therapy of proven value currently exists.

question: How do these exact processes ultimately restrict viral infectivity?
ground_truth: These processes ultimately restrict viral infectivity by triggering a legion of cellular defense and antiviral systems, inducing inflammatory factors and activating innate and adaptive immunity. Additionally, these mechanisms include the activation of direct antiviral activity against the replicative stages of viruses, targeting common pathways or structures that are essential for viral replication. This evolutionary arms race between countermeasures and species-specific factors ultimately prevent effective viral envelope clustering, decrease fluidity and imposes rigidity, and induce an outward membrane curvature that opposes the forces exerted by viral fusion machinery, ultimately inhibiting the ins and outs of HIV replication and restricting viral infectivity.
all_answers: These processes ultimately restrict viral infectivity by triggering a legion of cellular defense and antiviral systems, inducing inflammatory factors and activating innate and adaptive immunity. Additionally, these mechanisms include the activation of direct antiviral activity against the replicative stages of viruses, targeting common pathways or structures that are essential for viral replication. This evolutionary arms race between countermeasures and species-specific factors ultimately prevent effective viral envelope clustering, decrease fluidity and imposes rigidity, and induce an outward membrane curvature that opposes the forces exerted by viral fusion machinery, ultimately inhibiting the ins and outs of HIV replication and restricting viral infectivity.
reference_context: These unavoidable processes are common to all enveloped viruses. Moreover, the lipid envelope is the one component of the virus particle that is not encoded by the virus itself. It is perhaps unsurprising that the mammalian host has evolved multiple antiviral mechanisms whose role is to inhibit viral replicative processes that are associated with entry and exit , necessitating either the evolution of directly encoded countermeasures by the virus, or other mechanisms of resistance or avoidance. Furthermore, these mechanisms are often regulated by type 1 interferons and pattern recognition responses, linking these factors to the wider antiviral immune response.

question: How do some respiratory viruses spread?
ground_truth: Some respiratory viruses, such as rhinoviruses and influenza A viruses, can spread through direct contact, indirect contact, respiratory droplets, or aerosols. For example, rhinoviruses can survive on hands for several hours, allowing for easy human-to-human transmission through contact with contaminated hands. Influenza A viruses can be transmitted through respiratory droplets or aerosols, with aerosol transmission allowing for infection at a greater distance from the source.

question: What is the basic reproduction number (R 0)?
ground_truth: The basic reproduction number (R 0) is a widely used epidemiological measurement that represents the average number of secondary infections generated by a single primary case in a fully susceptible population. If R 0 > 1, then the disease can invade an otherwise susceptible population and persist, whereas if R 0 < 1, the disease cannot successfully invade and will die out. It is used to measure the transmission potential of a given population and is important for assessing the effectiveness of different intervention strategies in controlling the spread of diseases.
all_answers: The basic reproduction number (R 0) is a widely used epidemiological measurement that represents the average number of secondary infections generated by a single primary case in a fully susceptible population. If R 0 > 1, then the disease can invade an otherwise susceptible population and persist, whereas if R 0 < 1, the disease cannot successfully invade and will die out. It is used to measure the transmission potential of a given population and is important for assessing the effectiveness of different intervention strategies in controlling the spread of diseases.
reference_context: The basic reproductive ratio-also known as the basic reproductive number, the basic reproduction number, the control reproduction number, or R 0 -is one of the foremost concepts in epidemiology . R 0 is the most widely used epidemiological measurement of the transmission potential in a given population .
